es: Agarra a kettlebell by its manole in both manos (agarre prono) and colócate de pie up recto. Mantén this recto posture durante todo the movimiento and permite tu brazos to hang recto hacia el suelo delante de tu torso. Adopta una amplio postura con tu toes slightly facing outwards, this will be tu posición inicial.

Inhala as desciende by flexionaing at tu rodillas and caderas. Remember to mantén tu rodillas detrás de tu toes and put most of tu peso through tu talones. Continúa in this descent hasta que tu thighs are either parallel to el suelo or just past.

Después de una breve pausa, exhala and simultaneously empuja through tu talones, thighs and caderas to drive tu torso espalda along the same path into la posición inicial mientras tiraing the kettlebell up hacia tu chin. Mantén tu codos higher than the kettlebell at all times and mantén it cerca de tu cuerpo.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve the kettlebell espalda along the same path in to its posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Precaución: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variaciones: Barbell Sumo Squats or Dumbbell Sumo Squats.

name: Kettlebell Goblet Squat
instructions: Take hold of a kettlebell by its handle in both hands (overhand grip) and stand up straight. Maintain this straight posture throughout the movement and keep your arms as close to your chest as possible. Adopt a wide stance with your toes slightly facing outwards, this will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend by bending at your knees. Remember to keep your knees behind your toes and put most of your weight through your heels. Continue in this descent until your thighs are either parallel to the floor or just past.
After a brief pause, exhale and push through your heels, thighs and hips to bring your torso back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Make sure to maintain posture as failure to do so could result in injury.
Variations: Sumo Dumbbell Squats or Barbell Side Lunges.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Snatch
instructions: Grab a kettlebell with your right hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebell with a neutral grip (palm facing your pocket) and stand upright with the weight by your side. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the kettlebell between your legs and rotate it so you have an overhand grip (palm facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the kettlebell above your head until your arm is nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the kettlebell so that your palm is facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearm.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell along the same path back between your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Double Kettlebell Snatch or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

name: Kettlebell Pirate Ships
instructions: With a wide stance, hold a kettlebell with both hands. Allow it to hang at waist level with your arms extended. This will be your starting position.
Initiate the movement by turning to one side, swinging the kettlebell to head height. Briefly pause at the top of the motion.
Allow the bell to drop as you rotate to the opposite side, again raising the kettlebell to head height.
Repeat for the desired amount of repetitions.

name: Single Leg Kettlebell Deadlifts
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your left hand with a neutral grip (palm facing inwards) and adopt a single leg stance on your right leg. This will be your starting position.
To initiate this movement, inhale and slowly lean your torso forward and push the kettlebell down towards the floor, maintaining a straight back.
As your descend your torso towards the floor, lift your left leg up behind you and keep your right leg straight.
Once the kettlebell is an inch off the floor, exhale and return your torso along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Dumbbell Stiff Leg Dead Lifts or Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlifts.

To initiate this movimiento, inhala and lentamente inclínate tu torso hacia delante and empuja the kettlebell down hacia el suelo, maintaining a recto espalda.
As tu desciende tu torso hacia el suelo, eleva tu izquierdo pierna up detrás de and mantén tu derecho pierna recto.
Once the kettlebell is an inch off el suelo, exhala and vuelve tu torso along the same path espalda into its posición inicial.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Jerk
instructions: Grab a kettlebell and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebell up to your shoulder and allow it to rest against the back of your forearm. At this point, your elbow should be bent to 90 degrees and flared out wide. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement is to descend into a squatting motion by bending at your knees and hips. Once you get half way down, drive upward through your heels and quads in order to start elevating your torso and kettlebell, exhale as you do so.

Generate enough force so that you jump off the floor and press the kettlebell above your head until your elbow is locked out. Simultaneously, land into a deep squatting position to cushion the downward force. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Return your body to a standing position with your arm and kettlebell extended towards the ceiling. After a brief pause, return the kettlebell back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap arms.
Variations: Clean and Jerk or Kettlebell Clean and Jerk.

name: Kettlebell Front Squats
instructions: Take hold of a kettlebell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other) so that they are running down the back of each forearm. Keep them close to your body throughout the movement.
Adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance with your toes gently pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the kettlebells to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and if you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variation: Barbell Front Squats.

es: Agarra a kettlebell in each mano with a agarre neutro (palmaas enfrentadas) so that they are running down la espalda of each antebrazo. Mantén them cerca de tu cuerpo durante todo the movimiento.

Adopta una slightly amplior than anchura de hombros postura con tu toes suavemente pointing outward. Mantén a recto espalda durante todo the movimiento and mantén tu cabeza up. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Inhala and begin to desciende by flexionaing at tu rodillas and caderas. tu peso should be put predominantly through tu talones as siéntate espalda into the movimiento hasta que tu thighs are either parallel or just past to el suelo.

Después de una breve pausa, exhala and vuelve the kettlebells to la posición inicial along the same path, empuja through tu talones with the fuerza being driven through tu cuádriceps to do so. Colócate de pie to attention con tu piernas completamente extiendeed, espalda recto and hombros relajado.

name: Kettlebell Figure Of 8
instructions: Grab and place a kettlebell of the desired weight between your legs. Bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. At this point, the kettlebell should be directly under your chest as your right arm takes hold of the handle and is perpendicular to the floor. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, gently lift the kettlebell off the floor and swing it between your legs whilst receiving the handle in your left hand behind your left leg. Once you have done this, bring the kettlebell around the outside of your left leg. Continue to wrap around your leg until you bring the kettlebell between your legs where you then pass the handle over to your right hand (reaching from being your right leg).

Similarly, bring the weight around the outside of your right leg until you find yourself between your legs once more. Pass the kettlebell over to your left hand which is reaching from behind your left leg.
This will form a 'Figure of 8' pattern around your legs. Keep your breathing consistent throughout the movement and keep your back as straight as possible to reduce the risk of injury.
Continue in this manner until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Kettlebell Swings.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Dead Cleans
instructions: Grab a kettlebell and place it on the floor between your wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebell with an overhand grip. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, bend at your knees and hips (more so with your knees than hips) descending into a squat position whilst maintaining a straight back. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point.
Next, drive through your heels and quads and start elevating the kettlebell towards your shoulder.
Next you need to drive your pelvis forward when the kettlebell gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the kettlebell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the kettlebell further.
Once you are in a standing position, flip the kettlebell over your wrist so that it is resting on the back of your forearm.
Whilst maintaining good posture, bring the kettlebell back down along the same path to its starting position, reset, and go again!
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cleans or Dumbbell Cleans.

To initiate the movement, bend at your knees and hips (more so with your knees than hips) descending into a squat position whilst maintaining a straight back. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point.

es: Coge a kettlebell and coloca it on el suelo entre tu amplior than anchura de hombros postura. Agarra the kettlebell with an agarre prono. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, flexiona at tu rodillas and caderas (more so con tu rodillas than caderas) desciendeing into a squat posición mientras maintaining a recto espalda. tu pecho, hombros and cabeza should be facing hacia delante at this point.
A continuación, drive through tu talones and cuádriceps and start elevating the kettlebell hacia tu hombro.
A continuación debes drive tu pelvis hacia delante when the kettlebell gets to tu mid thigh. This coupled with the extensión through tu rodilla and tobillo joints, will propel the kettlebell arriba.
Once have thrusted tu caderas hacia delante, it is time to shrug as hard as can in order to eleva the kettlebell further.
Once are in a colócate de pieing posición, flip the kettlebell over tu wrist so that it is resting on la espalda of tu antebrazo.
mientras maintaining good posture, lleva the kettlebell espalda down along the same path to its posición inicial, reset, and go again!
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Cinclínates or Dumbbell Cinclínates.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Clean & Jerk
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in one hand, with a slightly wider than shoulder width overhand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arm to hang perpendicular to the floor until the kettlebell is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the kettlebell. Your back should remain straight and your arm should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the kettlebell gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propel the kettlebell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the kettlebell further. Flare your elbow up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the kettlebell and rotate your elbow under and in so that the kettlebell flips up and over your wrist to rest on the back of your forearm. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to jump into a split stance (top of a lunge). At the same time as powering the body into a jump, aggressively press the kettlebell above your head until your elbow is locked out.
Step forward with your rear leg so that you are now positioned under the kettlebell. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the kettlebell back into the starting position by reversing the upward motion, making sure you keep the kettlebell close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Variation: Clean & Jerk or Dumbbell Clean.

es: Coge a kettlebell in one mano, with a slightly amplior than anchura de hombros agarre prono. Flexiona at tu rodillas and caderas (more so con tu caderas than rodillas), maintaining a recto espalda and permiteing tu brazo to hang perpendicular to el suelo hasta que the kettlebell is just below tu rodillas. tu pecho, hombros and cabeza should be facing hacia delante at this point. Esta será tu posición inicial.

tu first movimiento will be to drive through tu talones and cuádriceps in order to start elevating the kettlebell. tu espalda should remain recto and tu brazo should still be perpendicular to el suelo at this point.

A continuación comes the acceleration from tu pelvis, debes drive tu pelvis hacia delante when the kettlebell gets to tu mid thigh. This coupled with the extensión through tu rodilla and tobillo joints, will propel the kettlebell arriba.
Once have thrusted tu caderas hacia delante, it is time to shrug as hard as can in order to eleva the kettlebell further. Flare tu codo up and outward hasta que tu cuerpo is at full extensión.

When at full extensión, aggressively squat under the kettlebell and rota tu codo under and in so that the kettlebell flips up and over tu wrist to rest on la espalda of tu antebrazo. Continúa to squat hasta que are comfortable to drive arriba through tu talones, cuádriceps and glúteos.
Mantén driving arriba hasta que are in a colócate de pieing posición.

Once are in a colócate de pieing posición, flexiona at tu rodillas to generate power to jump into a separar postura (parte superior of a zancada). At the same time as powering the cuerpo into a jump, aggressively press the kettlebell por encima de tu cabeza hasta que tu codo is locked out.
Step hacia delante con tu rear pierna so that are now posicióned under the kettlebell. Consejo: Manténing a recto espalda durante todo is parcantidad.
Descend the kettlebell espalda into la posición inicial by reversing the arriba motion, making sure mantén the kettlebell cerca de tu cuerpo durante todo and maintaining good posture.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones and swap lados.
Variación: Cinclínate & Jerk or Dumbbell Cinclínate.

name: Single Arm Kettlebell Cleans
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in your right hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebell with an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs) and stand upright with your arm hanging perpendicular to the floor in front of your body. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the kettlebell between your legs, you should feel as though you are pushing your hand towards the floor to emphasise the power generated in your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the kettlebell up towards shoulder height (your elbow should be flared out wide and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees)). As you do so, flip the kettlebell so that your palm is now facing forward with the weight running down the back of your forearm.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell along the same path back between your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Double Kettlebell Snatch or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

es: Coge a kettlebell in tu derecho mano and adopt a slightly amplior than anchura de hombros postura. Agarra the kettlebell with an agarre prono (palmaas mirando a los muslos) and colócate de pie erguido con tu brazo hanging perpendicular to el suelo delante de tu cuerpo. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, inhala and flexiona at tu rodillas and caderas so that tu thighs are parallel to el suelo. At the same time lleva the kettlebell entre tu piernas, should feel as though are empujaing tu mano hacia el suelo to emphasise the power generated in tu piernas.

Immediately after doing this, exhala and use this power to drive through tu talones, thighs and caderas to tira the kettlebell up hacia hombro height (tu codo should be flared out amplio and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees)). As do so, flip the kettlebell so that tu palma is now facing hacia delante with the peso running down la espalda of tu antebrazo.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve the kettlebell along the same path espalda entre tu piernas as squat into the next repetition.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variación: Double Kettlebell Snatch or Single Arm Dumbbell Cinclínate And Press.

name: Seated Kettlebell Arnold Press
instructions: Sit on a bench with the back support (just off) vertical. Hold two kettlebells with an overhand grip (palms facing forward) allowing them to rest on the back of your forearms.

To perform the movement, bring the kettlebells down towards your torso until you reach 90 degrees at the elbows. Rotate your wrists as you bring your elbows in towards each other, in front of your body. At this point your palms should be facing your torso. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.

After a brief pause, reverse the movement and press the kettlebells towards the ceiling, whilst rotating your palms back to the original position (facing forward). Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Seated Cable Shoulder Press.

es: Siéntate on a banco with la espalda support (just off) vertical. Hold two kettlebells with an agarre prono (palmaas hacia delante) permiteing them to rest on la espalda of tu antebrazos.

To perform the movimiento, lleva the kettlebells down hacia tu torso hasta que reach 90 degrees at the codos. Rota tu wrists as lleva tu codos in hacia each other, delante de tu cuerpo. At this point tu palmas should be facing tu torso. Inhala as perform this portion of the movimiento.

Después de una breve pausa, reverse the movimiento and press the kettlebells hacia el techo, mientras rotating tu palmas espalda to the original posición (facing hacia delante). Exhala as perform this portion of the movimiento.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Arnold Dumbbell Press or Seated Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Extended Range One-Arm Kettlebell Floor Press
instructions: Lie on the floor and position a kettlebell for one arm to press. The kettlebell should be held by the handle. The leg on the same side that you are pressing should be bent, with the knee crossing over the midline of the body.
Press the kettlebell by extending the elbow and adducting the arm, pressing it above your body. Return to the starting position.

name: Double Kettlebell Windmill
instructions: Place a kettlebell in front of your front foot and clean and press a kettlebell overhead with your opposite arm. Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder by extending through the legs and hips as you pull the kettlebell towards your shoulders. Rotate your wrist as you do so, so that the palm faces forward.

Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, push your butt out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell. Turn your feet out at a forty-five degree angle from the arm with the locked out kettlebell.
Bending at the hip to one side, sticking your butt out, slowly lean until you can retrieve the kettlebell from the floor. Keep your eyes on the kettlebell that you hold over your head at all times.
Pause for a second after retrieving the kettlebell from the ground and reverse the motion back to the starting position.

Keeping the kettlebell locked out at all times, push your butt out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell. Turn your feet out at a forty-five degree angle from the arm with the locked out kettlebell.

es: Coloca a kettlebell delante de tu delantero pie and cinclínate and press a kettlebell overcabeza con tu contrario brazo. Cinclínate the kettlebell to tu hombro by extiendeing through the piernas and caderas as tira the kettlebell hacia tu hombros. Rota tu wrist as do so, so that the palma faces hacia delante.

Manténing the kettlebell locked out at all times, empuja tu butt out in the direction of the locked out kettlebell. Turn tu pies out at a forty-five degree angle from the brazo with the locked out kettlebell.

Flexionaing at the cadera to one lado, sticking tu butt out, lentamente inclínate hasta que can retrieve the kettlebell from el suelo. Mantén tu eyes on the kettlebell that hold over tu cabeza at all times.
Haz una pausa for a second after retrieving the kettlebell from the ground and reverse the motion espalda to la posición inicial.

name: Double Kettlebell Split Jerk
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in both hands with an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and bring them up to your shoulders (the kettlebells should be resting on the back of your forearms at this point). Adopt a shoulder width standing stance with your elbows flared out wide. This will be your starting position.

To Initiate the movement, inhale and gently squat by bending at your knees and hips. Once you are half way down into a squatting position exhale and immediately press through your thighs and shoulders to elevate not only your arms (and therefore the kettlebells) into full extension but to jump off the floor slightly. This will allow you to split your stance and descend into a lunge position (90 degrees at both knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells along the same path, back into their starting position as you ascend your body into your original standing position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Kettlebell Jerk or Clean and Jerk.

Adopta una anchura de hombros colócate de pieing postura con tu codos flared out amplio. Esta será tu posición inicial.

To Initiate the movimiento, inhala and suavemente squat by flexionaing at tu rodillas and caderas. Once are half way down into a squatting posición exhala and immediately press through tu thighs and hombros to eleva not only tu brazos (and therefore the kettlebells) into full extensión but to jump off el suelo slightly. This will permite to separar tu postura and desciende into a zancada posición (90 degrees at both rodillas).
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve the kettlebells along the same path, espalda into their posición inicial as ascend tu cuerpo into tu original colócate de pieing posición.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Kettlebell Jerk or Cinclínate and Jerk.

name: Double Kettlebell Snatch
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebells with a neutral grip (palms facing together) and stand upright with the weights by your side. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and bend at your knees and hips so that your thighs are parallel to the floor. At the same time bring the kettlebells between your legs and rotate them so you have an overhand grip (palms facing behind you). You should feel as though you are pushing your hands towards the floor to emphasise the power generated by your legs.

Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels, thighs and hips to pull the kettlebells above your head until your arms are nearly locked out. As you do so, flip the kettlebells so that your palms are facing forward with the weights running down the back of your forearms.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells along the same path back between your legs as you squat into the next repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Barbell Clean and Press or Single Arm Dumbbell Clean And Press.

name: Double Kettlebell Push Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance. Bring the kettlebells up so that your hands are beside your ears, elbows are flared out wide and at an acute angle (less than 90 degrees). Make sure your palms are facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and gently bend at your knees and hips so that you create a small squat. Immediately after doing this, exhale and use this power to drive through your heels and thighs to press the kettlebells above your head until your arms are nearly locked out.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells back along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press or Cable Shoulder Press.

Adopt a shoulder width standing stance with your elbows flared out wide. This will be your starting position.

To Initiate the movement, inhale and gently squat by bending at your knees and hips. Once you are half way down into a squatting position exhale and immediately press through your thighs and shoulders to elevate not only your arms (and therefore the kettlebells) into full extension but to jump off the floor slightly. This will allow you to split your stance and descend into a lunge position (90 degrees at both knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebells along the same path, back into their starting position as you ascend your body into your original standing position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Kettlebell Jerk or Clean and Jerk.
es: Coge a kettlebell in both manos with an agarre prono (palmaas mirando en dirección contraria a ti) and lleva them up to tu hombros (the kettlebells should be resting on la espalda of tu antebrazos at this point). Adopta una anchura de hombros colócate de pieing postura con tu codos flared out amplio. Esta será tu posición inicial.

To Initiate the movimiento, inhala and suavemente squat by flexionaing at tu rodillas and caderas. Once are half way down into a squatting posición exhala and immediately press through tu thighs and hombros to eleva not only tu brazos (and therefore the kettlebells) into full extensión but to jump off el suelo slightly. This will permite to separar tu postura and desciende into a zancada posición (90 degrees at both rodillas).
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve the kettlebells along the same path, espalda into their posición inicial as ascend tu cuerpo into tu original colócate de pieing posición.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Kettlebell Jerk or Cinclínate and Jerk.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Renegade Rows
instructions: Place two kettlebells on the floor about shoulder width apart. Adopt an 'all-fours' position but instead of having your hands on the floor, take hold of the kettlebells with a neutral (palms facing each other) grip. At this point you should extend your legs out behind you so that your body is suspended above the floor in a straight line with your toes and the kettlebells being the only points of contact you have with the floor. The kettlebells should be directly below your chest so that your arms are perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

Whilst exhaling, row one of the kettlebells towards the side of your torso by squeezing the muscles in your back. Continue to pull your elbow as high as you can until your body wants to rotate. Keep your other arm stationary in the starting position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the opposite side and alternate between arms.
Continue in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Single Arm Rows or Alternating Dumbbell Renegade Rows.

The kettlebells should be directamente below tu pecho so that tu brazos are perpendicular to el suelo. Esta será tu posición inicial.

mientras exhaling, row one of the kettlebells hacia the lado of tu torso by squeezing the muscles in tu espalda. Continúa to tira tu codo as high as can hasta que tu cuerpo wants to rota. Mantén tu other brazo stationary in la posición inicial.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve the kettlebell espalda along the same path to la posición inicial.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Shoulder Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance (standing). Bring the kettlebells up to the side of your shoulders so your elbows are flared out wide and at a 90 degree angle, your palms should be facing forward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst exhaling, press one of the kettlebells towards the ceiling until your elbow becomes fully extended (straight). Keep your other arm stationary in the starting position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return the kettlebell back along the same path to the starting position.
Repeat for the opposite side and alternate between arms.
Continue in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternating Shoulder Press or Alternating Cable Shoulder Press.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Floor Press
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand with an overhand grip (palms facing away from you) and lay on your back. Have your arms extended towards the ceiling directly above your chest (the kettlebells should be resting on the back of your forearms at this point). Finally have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle whilst keeping your feet on the floor. This will be your starting position.
To Initiate the movement, inhale and descend one of the kettlebells down towards the floor. Allow your elbow to flare out wide until it gently touches the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the kettlebell along the same path, back into its starting position.
Repeat this movement in an alternating fashion between your left and right side until you have completed the prescribed amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternating Floor Press or Chest Press.

To Initiate the movimiento, inhala and desciende one of the kettlebells down hacia el suelo. Allow tu codo to abrirse amplio hasta que it suavemente touches el suelo.

Variaciones: Alternating Floor Press or Chest Press.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Hang Clean
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand and adopt a slightly wider than shoulder width stance. Take hold of the kettlebells with an overhand grip (palms facing your thighs) and stand upright with your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor in front of your body. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and bring one of the kettlebells up to shoulder height whilst relaxing your grip to allow the kettlebell to swing up and over to the back of your wrist. To do this, you will need to pull your elbow up toward the ceiling as fast as you can.

Immediately after doing this, take shallow breaths and return the kettlebell back along the same path back to its starting position. Whilst you are returning the first kettlebell back to its original position, pull your other kettlebell up to your shoulder. Once again, pull your elbow upwards as fast as you can and relax your grip to allow the kettlebell to flip over to the reverse side of your wrist.
Continue to alternate as you go and repeat this movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cleans or Dumbbell Cleans.

name: Alternating Kettlebell Bent Over Rows
instructions: Grab a kettlebell in each hand (palms facing your torso), bend your knees slightly and bring your torso forward by bending at your hips; as you bend make sure to keep your back straight until it is almost parallel to the floor. Tip: Make sure that you keep your spine in a neutral position. The weights should hang directly in front of you as your arms are perpendicular to the floor and your torso. This is your starting position.

While keeping the torso stationary, lift one of the kettlebells to your side (as you exhale), elevating your elbow towards the ceiling (do not exert any force with the forearm other than holding the weights). At the top contracted position, squeeze the back muscles and hold for a second.
Slowly lower the weight to the starting position as you inhale.
Once you have completed this movement, repeat for the opposite side.
Continue in this manner until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution:
This exercise is not recommended for people with back issues. A Low Cable Row is a better choice for people with back pain.
Ensure perfect form and never round the back as this can cause injury.
Be cautious with the weights you are using. If you are in doubt, use a lesser weight.
Variations: Dumbbell Bent Over Rows or Barbell Bent Over Rows.

name: Weighted Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle with your feet planted on the floor. Take hold of a weight plate and keep it close to your chest. Tip: For this movement you may either need a partner to hold your feet down or gently place additional weights on your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue to roll your shoulders towards your knees until your abdominals are fully contracted.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your torso back along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Rope Crunches or Crunches.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhala and roll tu hombros up and off the colchoneta by contracting tu músculos abdominales. Continúa to roll tu hombros hacia tu rodillas hasta que tu abdominales are completamente contracted.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve tu torso espalda along the same path espalda into its posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Rope Crunches or Crunches.

name: V Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down on your back. Have your arms extended above your head so they are just above the floor. Have your legs extended too, again just above the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate this movement, exhale and simultaneously elevate your legs up towards your torso and lift your torso and arms up towards your legs. At the top of the movement your abdominals should be fully contracted and you should be reaching for your toes.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your legs and torso along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Be careful of your lower back throughout this movement.
Variations: Cocoons or Jackknife Sit-Ups.

name: Tuck Crunches
instructions: To begin, lie down on the floor or an exercise mat with your back pressed against the floor.  Your arms should be lying across your sides with the palms facing down.

Your legs should be crossed by wrapping one ankle around the other.  Slowly elevate your legs up in the air until your thighs are perpendicular to the floor with a slight bend at the knees.  Note: Your knees and toes should be parallel to the floor as opposed to the thighs.
Move your arms from the floor and cross them so they are resting on your chest.  This is the starting position.
While keeping your lower back pressed against the floor, slowly lift your torso.  Remember to exhale while perform this part of the exercise.
Slowly begin to lower your torso back down to the starting position while inhaling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Variations: This exercise can be performed with a weighted plate resting across your chest with your arms holding it.  This can be a bit challenging for those who have never performed this exercise with weights. Always try the exercise without weights before moving to weighted plates.
You can also perform this exercise with your hands clasped behind your head.  This is a great way to add variety to your routine.

name: Toe Touches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend your arms above your head with your legs straight, pointing directly towards the ceiling (you can cross your legs if you wish). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst reaching towards your toes. Continue with this movement until your fingers touch your toes or your abdominal muscles are as tight as possible.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Rope Crunches.

name: Sit Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is vertical to the floor and your chest is very close to your knees.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cable Crunches.

name: Side Bridges
instructions: Grab a mat and place it on the floor, lay on your side with your shoulders elevated from the floor by leaning on your forearm. Have your other arm placed gently across your chest for stability (you can also place it on the floor in front of you if youy need further support). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and lift your pelvis directly upward towards the ceiling. Your body should now be in one straight line from your head to toes. The two points of contact with the floor should now be your bottom foot and your forearm (you can also perform this movement from your knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Plank With A Twist.

en: Grab a mat and place it on the floor, lay on your side with your shoulders elevated from the floor by leaning on your forearm. Have your other arm placed gently across your chest for stability (you can also place it on the floor in front of you if youy need further support). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and lift your pelvis directly upward towards the ceiling. Your body should now be in one straight line from your head to toes. The two points of contact with the floor should now be your bottom foot and your forearm (you can also perform this movement from your knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Plank With A Twist.
es: Coge a colchoneta and coloca it on el suelo, lay on tu lado con tu hombros elevad from el suelo by inclínateing on tu antebrazo. Have tu other brazo colocad suavemente across tu pecho for stability ( can also coloca it on el suelo delante de if youy need further support). Esta será tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhala and eleva tu pelvis directamente arriba hacia el techo. tu cuerpo should now be in one recto line from tu cabeza to toes. The two points of contact with el suelo should now be tu parte baja pie and tu antebrazo ( can also perform this movimiento from tu rodillas).
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve tu cuerpo espalda along the same path into la posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variación: Plank With A Twist.

name: Flat Bench Leg Pull In
instructions: Grab a bench and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the bench by the side of your head. Your knees should be directly above your pelvis with both your hips and knees bent at a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and extend your knee and hip joints so that your legs are just above parallel to the floor. Continue to extend until your legs are out straight, making sure your lower back is not under any stress or strain.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Cable Crunches.

After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

es: Coge a banco and lay flat on tu espalda, tu brazos should be holding onto the banco by the lado of tu cabeza. tu rodillas should be directamente por encima de tu pelvis with both tu caderas and rodillas bent at a 90 degree angle. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, inhala and extiende tu rodilla and cadera joints so that tu piernas are just por encima de parallel to el suelo. Continúa to extiende hasta que tu piernas are out recto, making sure tu zona lumbar is not under any stress or strain.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala and vuelve tu piernas espalda along the same path into la posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Cocoons or Cable Crunches.

name: Scissor Kicks
instructions: Grab a mat and lay flat on your back, your arms should run by the side of your torso with your forearms and hands placed just so, under your hips and glutes to support your lower back. At this point you should inhale and lift your feet around 6 inches off of the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, simultaneously take shallow breaths whilst kicking your feet up and down between a vertical position and a few inches from the floor (as if you were treading water). When you do this, use the whole of your legs rather than just from your knees down. Therefore the movement should be coming from your hips. Tip: If you need to adjust your hands, drop your feet to the floor and start the movement again in order to preserve your lower back.
Continue in the fashion until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back issues, this may not be the best exercise to perform.
Variations: Hip Raises or Flutter Kicks.

name: Reverse Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your hands gently placed on your temples with your knees and hips bent at a 90 degree angle. Your thighs should be vertical from the floor and lower legs parallel to the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your knees in towards your torso by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue with this movement until your knees are as close to your shoulders as they can be.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Flat Bench Leg Pull-In or Cocoons.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhala and tira tu rodillas in hacia tu torso by contracting tu músculos abdominales. Continúa with this movimiento hasta que tu rodillas are as cerca de tu hombros as they can be.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve tu cuerpo along the same path espalda to la posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Flat Bench Leg Tira-In or Cocoons.

name: Push Up to Side Plank
instructions: To start this exercise kneel down on the floor, and lean forward so that you are now weight baring on your arms. Have your hands placed just outside of where your chest would be. Now you should come up onto your feet so that your body is suspended above the floor, the only points of contact (with the floor) should be your hands and feet. Your body should be in one straight line from head to toe and your arms perpendicular to the floor under your chest. This will be your starting position.

Whilst inhaling, slowly lower your chest towards the ground whist flaring your elbows out wide. When you are about an inch or so from the ground, you should have created a triangle with your hand positions and your eye line.

Pause for a brief movement, exhale and press through your palms to return your body along the same path to the starting position. Tip: Squeeze your pectoral muscles as you do so and when you get to the top of the movement, do not lock your elbows out.

Next, Whilst still exhaling, lift one of your hands off the floor whilst roating your torso. You should continue to rotate until your chest is perpendicular to the floor (facing the wall rather than the floor) creating a side on 'T' position.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your torso back along the same path into the starting position.
You can either complete all repetitions on one side first or alternate as you go.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Plank With Twist or Plank.

name: V Sit With Plate Rotation
instructions: Grab a weight plate and take a seat on the floor. Hold the plate above your hips (flexing slightly at the elbow) and create an imaginary 'V' shape with your body by leaning back and lifting your feet off the floor. Tip: Your knees should be bent and if any pressure comes through your lower back, replace your feet back onto the floor. This is the starting position.
Twist your torso to the right hand side until the plate is just off the floor. Exhale as you twist, making sure that your head follows your shoulders.
Reverse the movement towards the left side keeping your breathing constant and exhaling as the weight is lowered either side.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back pain, this may not be the most suitable exercise.
Variations: Cable Russian Twists or V-Sit Twist With Med Ball.

name: Plank
instructions: Firstly, place a mat on the floor underneath your torso as you adopt an 'all-fours' position above it. From here you will need to bend your elbows to a 90 degree angle and place your upper body weight onto your forearms. After you have done that, take a step backwards with your feet and place your remaining bodyweight through your toes. At this point your body should be suspended (in a straight line) above the floor with just your forearms and toes as points of contact. This will be your starting position.

Keep your abdominal muscles tight throughout this exercise and keep your pelvis in line with the rest of your body to minimise risk of injury to your lower back (you can elevate your pelvis slightly to reduce lower back pain).
Hold this position for as long as you can or for the recommended amount of time.

Mantén tu músculos abdominales tight durante todo this exercise and mantén tu pelvis in line with the rest of tu cuerpo to minimise risk of injury to tu zona lumbar ( can eleva tu pelvis slightly to reduce zona lumbar pain).
Hold this posición for as long as can or for the recommended cantidad of time.

name: Otis Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Have your knees bent to create a 90 degree angle with your feet planted on the floor. Take hold of a weight plate and keep it close to your chest. Tip: For this movement you may either need a partner to hold your feet down or gently place additional weights on your feet. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst pressing the weight plate directly towards the ceiling at the same time. Continue to sit up as you push the weight as high towards the ceiling as possible.
Once your arms are vertical and your abdominal muscles are as tight as they can be, pause briefly, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Rope Crunches or Cocoons.

Once tu brazos are vertical and tu músculos abdominales are as tight as they can be, pause briefly, inhala and vuelve tu cuerpo espalda along the same path into la posición inicial.

name: Oblique Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles whilst rotating your torso at the same time, aiming one shoulder to the opposite knee.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path to the original position.
Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cross Body Crunches or Rope Oblique Crunches.

name: Leg Pull Ins
instructions: Grab a flat bench, take a seat on it and grip the bench with your hands behind your back. At this point you should bend at your knees and hips in order to tuck your knees in as close to your chest as possible. Lean your torso forward into your knees to create optimal contraction within your abdominal muscles. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and simultaneously extend your knee and hip joints so that your legs are just above parallel to the floor whilst leaning your torso backwards to lengthen your abdominals. Your arms should be taking as little weight as possible so that your core is working at it's maximum, this is at your discretion.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your torso and legs back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Flat Bench Leg Pull Ins.

name: Hanging Pike
instructions: Grab a pull-up bar with a shoulder width, neutral grip (palms facing each other). As you have both arms extended above your head, maintain a straight back as you lift your feet to allow your body to hang from the bar. Bend at your knees in front of your body so they and your hips are at 90 degrees. This is your starting position. Tip: For those of you that have a stronger core, try straightening your legs and maintain that extension throughout.

To initiate the movement, exhale and activate your abdominals to bring your knees up towards your shoulders in a semi-circular motion as your hips tilt underneath your body. Continue to bring your legs up towards the ceiling until they between your hands. Be sure to keep your arms at full extension throughout the movement.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your legs and torso along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Cable Reverse Crunches.

en: abdominals
es: abdominales

As have both brazos extiendeed por encima de tu cabeza, maintain a recto espalda as eleva tu pies to permite tu cuerpo to hang from the bar. Flexiona at tu rodillas delante de tu cuerpo so they and tu caderas are at 90 degrees. Esta es tu posición inicial. Consejo: For those of that have a stronger core, try rectoening tu piernas and maintain that extensión durante todo.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhala and activate tu abdominales to lleva tu rodillas up hacia tu hombros in a semi-movimiento circular as tu caderas tilt debajo de tu cuerpo. Continúa to lleva tu piernas up hacia el techo hasta que they entre tu manos. Be sure to mantén tu brazos at full extensión durante todo the movimiento.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and desciende tu piernas and torso along the same path espalda into their posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Hanging Leg Elevas or Cable Reverse Crunches.

name: Hanging Leg Raises
instructions: Take hold of a chin up bar/handles and allow your body to hang perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and elevate your legs in front of your body by contracting your abdominal muscles (either keeping them as straight as possible, or bending at the knees) until they create a 90 degree angle with your torso (if you have bent your knees, you should have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your legs back along the same path into the starting position. Tip: This movement should be slow and controlled so that you do not swing.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hip Raises or Flat Bench Leg Pull Ins.

en: abdominals
es: abdominales

To initiate the movement, exhale and elevate your legs in front of your body by contracting your abdominal muscles (either keeping them as straight as possible, or bending at the knees) until they create a 90 degree angle with your torso (if you have bent your knees, you should have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your legs back along the same path into the starting position. Tip: This movement should be slow and controlled so that you do not swing.

Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve tu piernas espalda along the same path into la posición inicial. Consejo: This movimiento should be lento and controlled so that do not swing.

Variaciones: Hip Elevas or Flat Bench Leg Tira Ins.

name: Gorilla Pull Ups
instructions: Grab a pull-up bar with a slightly wider than shoulder width underhand grip (palms facing your torso). As you have both arms extended above your head, maintain a straight back as you lift your feet to allow your body to hang from the bar. Bend at your knees (90 degrees) so that your ankles are behind your body. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your torso up towards the ceiling through the muscles in your back and biceps. As you are ascending, activate your abdominals to bring your knees up towards your shoulders as your hips tilt underneath your body. Continue to ascend in this fashion until your chest is as close to your hands as possible and your knees close to your chest.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your body along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Hanging Pikes.

en: Grab a pull-up bar with a slightly wider than shoulder width underhand grip (palms facing your torso). As you have both arms extended above your head, maintain a straight back as you lift your feet to allow your body to hang from the bar. Bend at your knees (90 degrees) so that your ankles are behind your body. This is your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and pull your torso up towards the ceiling through the muscles in your back and biceps. As you are ascending, activate your abdominals to bring your knees up towards your shoulders as your hips tilt underneath your body. Continue to ascend in this fashion until your chest is as close to your hands as possible and your knees close to your chest.
After a brief pause, inhale and descend your body along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Hanging Pikes.
es: Coge a tira-up bar with a slightly amplior than anchura de hombros undermano grip (palmas facing tu torso). As have both brazos extiendeed por encima de tu cabeza, maintain a recto espalda as eleva tu pies to permite tu cuerpo to hang from the bar. Flexiona at tu rodillas (90 degrees) so that tu tobillos are detrás de tu cuerpo. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhala and tira tu torso up hacia el techo through the muscles in tu espalda and bíceps. As are ascending, activate tu abdominales to lleva tu rodillas up hacia tu hombros as tu caderas tilt debajo de tu cuerpo. Continúa to ascend in this fashion hasta que tu pecho is as cerca de tu manos lo máximo posible and tu rodillas cerca de tu pecho.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and desciende tu cuerpo along the same path espalda into its posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Hanging Leg Elevas or Hanging Pikes.

name: Frog Sit Ups
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. Next, place the soles of your shoes together and allow your knees to relax out wide. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your feet by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your abdominals are as tight as possible.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Sit-Ups or Otis-Ups.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhala and curl tu hombros up and off the colchoneta hacia tu pies by contracting tu músculos abdominales. Continúa in this fashion hasta que tu abdominales are as tight lo máximo posible.

Variaciones: Siéntate-Ups or Otis-Ups.

name: Flutter Kicks
instructions: Grab a mat and lay flat on your back, your arms should run by the side of your torso with your forearms and hands placed just so, under your hips and glutes to support your lower back. At this point you should inhale and lift your feet around 6 inches off of the floor. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, simultaneously take shallow breaths whilst kicking your feet up and down by no more than 12 inches (as if you were treading water). When you do this, use the whole of your legs rather than just from your knees down. Therefore the movement should be coming from your hips. Tip: If you need to adjust your hands, drop your feet to the floor and start the movement again in order to preserve your lower back.
Continue in the fashion until you have completed the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from lower back issues, this may not be the best exercise to perform.
Variations: Hip Raises or Flat Bench Leg Pull Ins.

name: Flat Bench Lying Leg Raises
instructions: Grab a bench and lay flat on your back, your arms should be holding onto the bench by the side of your head. Elevate your legs (at full extension) vertically towards the ceiling until you create a 90 degree bend at your hips. Tip: Keep your legs extended throughout the movement. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and start to descend your legs on an arcing motion towards the floor. Continue to descend until your legs are parallel with the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your legs along the same path back into their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Hanging Leg Raises or Reverse Crunches.

Para iniciar el movimiento, inhala and start to desciende tu piernas on an arcing motion hacia el suelo. Continúa to desciende hasta que tu piernas are parallel with el suelo.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala and vuelve tu piernas along the same path espalda into their posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Hanging Leg Elevas or Reverse Crunches.

name: Decline Oblique Crunches
instructions: Find yourself a decline bench (around 30 degrees) and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and secure your legs so you don't slip down the bench (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the bench towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Twist your torso as you go so that your right elbow is aiming at your left knee. Continue in this fashion until you cannot contract your abdominals or obliques any more.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Alternate sides as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Kneeling Cable Crunches With Alternating Oblique Twists or Oblique Crunches.

name: Decline Crunches
instructions: Find yourself a decline bench (around 30 degrees) and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and secure your legs so you don't slip down the bench (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the bench towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is vertical to the floor and your chest is very close to your knees.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Cable Crunches.

name: Dead Bug
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend your arms directly above your chest towards the ceiling and maintain their position throughout. For your lower body, create a 90 degree bend at both your hips and knee joints so your lower legs are parallel with the floor, feet in mid air. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and extend one of your legs out and lower towards the floor as you go. Continue until your heel is an inch off the floor.
After a brief pause, exhale and return your leg back along the same path into the starting position.
Alternate as you go and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Reverse Crunches.

After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cocoons or Cable Crunches.

name: Crunches Hands Overhead
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend and place your arms on the floor above your head and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your abdominals cannot be any tighter. Keep your arms at full extension behind your head adding resistance to your crunches.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Rope Crunches.

name: Cross Body Crunches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, you need to simultaneously, bring your right knee up and in towards your body whilst crunching your left shoulder over towards it (exhale as you do this).
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path to the original position.
Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side. So exhale and bring your left knee up and in towards your torso whilst crunching your right shoulder over towards it.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Ab Bike.

name: Cocoons
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Extend your arms above your head with your legs laying flat on the floor. Your body should be at full extension. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and simultaneously pull your knees (bending them as you go) in towards your torso and roll your shoulders up and off the mat by contracting your abdominal muscles and bringing your arms towards your toes. Continue with this movement until your knees are as close to your shoulders as they can be.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Flat Bench Leg Pull-In or Jacknives.

Continúa with this movimiento hasta que tu rodillas are as cerca de tu hombros as they can be.

name: Bottoms Up
instructions: Grab a mat and lay flat on your back, your arms should be by your side with your legs extended. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and bring your knees in towards your chest by bending at both the knees and hips. Once they reach your chest you should extend your legs towards the ceiling so that they lift your hips and glutes up and off the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your legs along the same path back to the starting position.
Variation: Reverse Crunches or Cocoons.

es: abdominales

name: Alternating Heel Touches
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your chest and lift your legs so that you have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees. Crunch your abs so that you lift your shoulders up from the mat and you are ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and reach around the side of your body with your right hand towards your right heel, your legs should remain stationary. Tip: To acheive a peak contraction, crunch upward slightly as you go.
After a brief pause inhale and return your body along the same path back to the starting position.
Now repeat the movement for your left hand side. Exhale and reach around your body with your left arm towards your left heel. Inhale and return to the starting position.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: Weighted Side Bends.

name: Air Bike
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples and lift your legs so that you have a 90 degree angle at both your hips and knees. Crunch your abs so that you lift your shoulders up from the mat and you are ready to go. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, you need to simultaneously, bring your right knee up and in towards your body whilst crunching your left shoulder over towards it (exhale as you do this). As you do this extend your left leg to create space for your left elbow.

Gently inhale and return to the original position. Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side. So exhale and bring your left knee up and in towards your torso whilst crunching your right shoulder over towards it, extending your right leg as you go.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Kneeling Oblique Cable Crunches .

Gently inhale and return to the original position. Very briefly pause here and repeat for the opposite side. So exhale and bring your left knee up and in towards your torso whilst crunching your right shoulder over towards it, extending your right leg as you go.
Continue alternating in this manner until you complete the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variation: V Sit With Plate Rotation or Kneeling Oblique Cable Crunches .
es: Coge a colchoneta and lay down flat on tu espalda. Coloca tu manos suavemente on tu temples and eleva tu piernas so that have a 90 degree angle at both tu caderas and rodillas. Crunch tu abs so that eleva tu hombros up from the colchoneta and are ready to go. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, debes simultaneously, lleva tu derecho rodilla up and in hacia tu cuerpo mientras crunching tu izquierdo hombro over hacia it (exhala as do this). As do this extiende tu izquierdo pierna to create space for tu izquierdo codo.

Gently inhala and vuelve to the original posición. Very briefly pause here and repeat for the contrario lado. So exhala and lleva tu izquierdo rodilla up and in hacia tu torso mientras crunching tu derecho hombro over hacia it, extiendeing tu derecho pierna durante el movimiento.
Continúa alternating in this manner hasta que complete the número indicado de repeticiones.
Variación: V Siéntate With Plate Rotation or Kneeling Oblique Cable Crunches .

name: Ab Wheels Rollout
instructions: Grab an ab-wheel and place it on the floor at the end of a mat. Kneel down on the mat and adopt an overhand grip on the wheel. With a bend at your knees and hips, lean your bodyweight forward so that your (straight) arms are holding you upright. This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, inhale and slowly roll the wheel forward, stretching your arms out as far as you can whilst dropping your pelvis down at the same time.
After a brief pause, exhale and use your abdominal muscles to pull the wheel back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: If you suffer from any lower back pain or hernias, this exercise is not suitable.
Variation: Barbell Roll Out.

name: 3:4 Sit Up
instructions: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is 3/4 of the way towards being vertical to the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Crunches or Sit-Ups.

en: abdominals
es: abdominales

en: Grab a mat and lay down flat on your back. Place your hands gently on your temples/chest and bend your knees so that you have a 90 degree angle (you can tuck your feet under a couple of dumbbells for assistance). This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and curl your shoulders up and off the mat towards your knees by contracting your abdominal muscles. Continue in this fashion until your torso is 3/4 of the way towards being vertical to the floor.
After a brief pause, inhale and return your body back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

es: Coge a colchoneta and lay down flat on tu espalda. Coloca tu manos suavemente on tu temples/pecho and flexiona tu rodillas so that have a 90 degree angle ( can tuck tu pies under a couple of mancuernas for assipostura). Esta será tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhala and curl tu hombros up and off the colchoneta hacia tu rodillas by contracting tu músculos abdominales. Continúa in this fashion hasta que tu torso is 3/4 of the way hacia being vertical to el suelo.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala and vuelve tu cuerpo espalda along the same path into la posición inicial.
Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Crunches or Siéntate-Ups.

name: Banded Upright Rows
instructions: To begin, stand on an exercise band so that tension begins at arm's length. Grasp the handles using a pronated (palms facing your thighs) grip that is slightly less than shoulder width. The handles should be resting on top of your thighs. Your arms should be extended with a slight bend at the elbows and your back should be straight. This will be your starting position.Use your side shoulders to lift the handles as you exhale. The handles should be close to the body as you move them up. Continue to lift the handles until they nearly touches your chin. Tip: Your elbows should drive the motion. As you lift the handles, your elbows should always be higher than your forearms. Also, keep your torso stationary and pause for a second at the top of the movement.Lower the handles back down slowly to the starting position. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.Variations: This exercise can also be performed using a straight or e-z bar. Another variation is to use dumbbells, though this later exercise should be reserved by the most advanced people that are well familiarized with correct execution.

en: traps
es: trapecios

The handles should be resting on top of your thighs. Your arms should be extended with a slight bend at the elbows and your back should be straight. This will be your starting position.Use your side shoulders to lift the handles as you exhale. The handles should be close to the body as you move them up. Continue to lift the handles until they nearly touches your chin. Tip: Your elbows should drive the motion. As you lift the handles, your elbows should always be higher than your forearms. Also, keep your torso stationary and pause for a second at the top of the movement.Lower the handles back down slowly to the starting position. Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.Variations: This exercise can also be performed using a straight or e-z bar. Another variation is to use dumbbells, though this later exercise should be reserved by the most advanced people that are well familiarized with correct execution.
es: To begin, colócate de pie on an exercise banda so that tensión begins at brazo's length. Grasp the manoles using a pronated (palmaas mirando a los muslos) grip that is slightly less than anchura de hombros. The manoles should be resting on parte superior of tu thighs. tu brazos should be extiendeed with a slight flexiona at the codos and tu espalda should be recto. Esta será tu posición inicial.Use tu lado hombros to eleva the manoles as exhala. The manoles should be cerca de the cuerpo as move them up. Continúa to eleva the manoles hasta que they nearly touches tu chin. Consejo: tu codos should drive the motion. As eleva the manoles, tu codos should always be higher than tu antebrazos. Also, mantén tu torso stationary and pause for a second at the parte superior of the movimiento.Baja the manoles espalda down lentamente to la posición inicial. Inhala as perform this portion of the movimiento.Repite el número indicado de repeticiones.Variaciones: This exercise can also be performed using a recto or e-z bar. Another variation is to use mancuernas, though this later exercise should be reserved by the most advanced people that are well familiarized with correct execution.

name: Speed Band Pushdown
instructions: Choke a band onto a pull up bar.
Grasp the band with a pronated grip, hands close together.
Beginning with the elbow flexed, extend the elbow to straighten your arms, ensuring you keep your upper arm in place.
Pause briefly, and return to the starting position.

en: triceps

es: Choke a banda onto a tira up bar.
Grasp the banda with a agarre prono, manos cerca degether.
Beginning with the codo flexed, extiende the codo to rectoen tu brazos, ensuring mantén tu upper brazo in coloca.
Haz una pausa briefly, and vuelve to la posición inicial.

name: Dynaband Overhead Tricep Extensions
instructions: For this exercise anchor a band to the ground. We used an incline bench and anchored the band to the base, standing over the bench. Alternatively, this could be performed standing on the band.
To begin, pull the band behind your head, holding it with a pronated grip and your elbows up. This will be your starting position.
To perform the movement, extend through the elbow to to straighten your arms, ensuring that you keep your upper arm in place.
Pause, and then return to the starting position.

To begin, tira the banda detrás de tu cabeza, holding it with a agarre prono and tu codos up. Esta será tu posición inicial.
To perform the movimiento, extiende through the codo to to rectoen tu brazos, ensuring that mantén tu upper brazo in coloca.

name: Dynaband Shoulder Press
instructions: To begin, stand on an exercise band so that tension begins at arm's length.  Grasp the handles and lift them so that the hands are at shoulder height at each side.
Rotate the wrists so that the palms of your hands are facing forward.  Your elbows should be bent, with the upper arms and forearms in line to the torso.  This is your starting position.
As you exhale, lift the handles up until your arms are fully extended overhead.

name: Seated Banded Hamstring Curls
instructions: Secure a band close to the ground and place a bench a couple feet away from it.
Seat yourself on the bench and secure the band behind your ankles, beginning with your legs straight. This will be your starting position.
Flex the knees, bringing your feet towards the bench. You may need to lean back slightly to keep your feet from striking the floor.
Pause at the completion of the movement, and then slowly return to the starting position.
