name: Barbell Front Squats Cross Grip
instructions: Set the height of the pins in a squat rack so that the barbell can lay across your shoulders comfortably. Next, set the safety bars at the desired height (typically waist high) so that if you fail on a rep, they will take the weight and you will be safe. Load up the barbell with the correct amount of weight on it and you are now ready to go.

Step underneath the bar and take the weight across the front of your shoulders. You can either relax your wrists and place then under the bar to secure its position or you can cross your arms over and lift your elbows, which ever is most comfortable for yourself. Maintain a straight back and a small bend at your knees when unracking the bar. Press through your legs to lift the bar up and take a small step backwards into the middle of the rack.
Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes slightly pointing outward. Maintain a straight back throughout the movement and keep your head up. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and begin to descend by bending at your knees and hips. Your weight should be put predominantly through your heels as you sit back into the movement until your thighs are either parallel or just past to the floor.

After a brief pause, exhale and return the bar to the starting position along the same path, push through your heels with the force being driven through your quads to do so. Stand to attention with your legs fully extended, back straight and shoulders relaxed.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is a very complex movement and will require a spotter. If you do not have a spotter, be cautious with your weights. If you suffer from back issues, this exercise may not be suitable.
Variations: Barbell Squats or Kettlebell Squats.

name: Zercher Squat
instructions: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes.  To begin, first set the bar on a rack that best matches your height.  The correct height should be anywhere above the waist but below the chest. Once the correct height is chosen and the bar is loaded, lock your hands together and place the bar on top of your arms in between the forearm and upper arm.
Lift the bar up so that it is resting on top of your forearms.  If you are holding the bar properly, it should look as if you have your arms crossed but with a bar running across them.

Step away from the rack and position your legs using a shoulder width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out.  Keep your head up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight back.  This will be your starting position.  (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the foot stances section).

Begin to lower the bar by bending the knees as you maintain a straight posture with the head up.  Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the calves becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the floor).  Inhale as you perform this portion of the movement.  Tip: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the knees should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front.  If your knees are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the knee and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Begin to raise the bar as you exhale by pushing the floor with the ball of your foot mainly as you straighten the legs again and go back to the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Caution: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your arms when lifting the barbell.  It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form.  Also, make sure that your back is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.

es: This exercise is best performed inside a squat rack for safety purposes.  To begin, first set the barra on a rack that best matches your height.  The correct height should be anywhere above the waist but below the pecho. Once the correct height is chosen and the barra is loaded, lock your manos together and place the barra on top of your brazos in between the forebrazo and upper brazo.
Lift the barra up so that it is resting on top of your forebrazos.  If you are holding the barra properly, it should look as if you have your brazos crossed but with a barra running across them.

Step away from the rack and posición your legs using a hombro width medium stance with the toes slightly pointed out.  Mantén your cabeza up at all times as looking down will get you off balance and also maintain a straight espalda.  Esta será la posición inicial.  (Note: For the purposes of this discussion we will use the medium stance described above which targets overall development; however you can choose any of the three stances discussed in the pie stances section).

Empieza to lower the barra by bending the rodillas as you maintain a straight posture with the cabeza up.  Continue down until the angle between the upper leg and the gemelos becomes slightly less than 90-degrees (which is the point in which the upper legs are below parallel to the suelo).  Inspira as you perform this portion of the movimiento.  Consejo: If you performed the exercise correctly, the front of the rodillas should make an imaginary straight line with the toes that is perpendicular to the front.  If your rodillas are past that imaginary line (if they are past your toes) then you are placing undue stress on the rodilla and the exercise has been performed incorrectly.
Empieza to raise the barra as you espira by pushing the suelo with the ball of your pie mainly as you straighten the legs again and go espalda to the posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.

Precaución: This exercise is not to be taken lightly as you are applying a lot of pressure to your brazos when lifting the barrara.  It is always a good idea to have a spotter as they can help you keep proper form.  Also, make sure that your espalda is kept straight as bending it can cause an injury.

name: Standing Narrow Grip EZ Bar Overhead Tricep Extensions
instructions: Grab a barbell with an overhand, shoulder width grip and press towards the ceiling until your elbows are locked out. Adopt a standing, shoulder width stance with your back straight, barbell directly above your head. Tip: Keep your elbows as close together as you can throughout to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.

Inhale and descend the barbell on a semi-circular motion down towards the back of your head. Keep the upper arms still and control this movement through bending your elbows and stretching your triceps. Remember to keep your elbows tucked in.
Pause for a brief movement once you have created an acute angle at your elbows. Then pull through your triceps to take the barbell back along the same path to your starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Overhead Tricep Extension (Rope On Cables) or Barbell Skullcrushers (Incline).

es: Coge a barrara with an overmano, hombro width grip and press hacia the techo until your codos are locked out. Adopt a standing, hombro width stance with your espalda straight, barrara directly above your cabeza. Consejo: Mantén your codos as close together as you can throughout to initiate maximum tricep activity. Esta será la posición inicial.

Inspira and descend the barrara on a semi-circular motion down hacia the espalda of your cabeza. Mantén the upper brazos still and control this movimiento through bending your codos and stretching your tríceps. Remember to keep your codos tucked in.
Pause for a brief movimiento once you have created an acute angle at your codos. Then pull through your tríceps to take the barrara espalda along the same path to your posición inicial.
Repite el número de repeticiones recomendado.
Variaciones: Overcabeza Tricep Extension (Rope On Poleas) or barra Skullcrushers (Incline).

name: Frankenstein Squats
instructions: This drill teaches you the proper positioning of both the bar and your body during the clean and front squat.

Place the barbell on the front of the shoulders, releasing your grip and extending your arms out in front of you. The shoulders should be pushed forward to create a shelf, and the bar should be in contact with the throat. Ensure that you only move your shoulder blades forward; don't round the thoracic spine.

Squat by flexing the knees and hips, sitting in between your legs. Keep the torso upright, the arms up, and the shoulders forward, and the bar should stay in place. Go to the bottom of the squat, until your hamstrings contact your calves.
Return to the upright position by driving through the front of the heel and extending the knees and hips.

en: This drill teaches you the proper positioning of both the bar and your body during the clean and front squat.

Place the barbell on the front of the shoulders, releasing your grip and extending your arms out in front of you. The shoulders should be pushed forward to create a shelf, and the bar should be in contact with the throat. Ensure that you only move your shoulder blades forward; don't round the thoracic spine.

Squat by flexing the knees and hips, sitting in between your legs. Keep the torso upright, the arms up, and the shoulders forward, and the bar should stay in place. Go to the bottom of the squat, until your hamstrings contact your calves.
Return to the upright position by driving through the front of the heel and extending the knees and hips.
es: Este ejercicio te enseña la posición adecuada tanto de la barra como de tu cuerpo durante la cargada y la sentadilla frontal.

Coloca la barra en la parte delantera de los hombros, soltando el agarre y extendiendo los brazos frente a ti. Los hombros deben estar empujados hacia adelante para crear un soporte y la barra debe estar en contacto con la garganta. Asegúrate de mover solo los omóplatos hacia adelante; no redondees la columna torácica.

Ponte en cuclillas flexionando las rodillas y las caderas, sentándote entre las piernas. Mantén el torso erguido, los brazos arriba y los hombros hacia adelante, y la barra debe permanecer en su lugar. Baja al final de la sentadilla, hasta que los isquiosurales entren en contacto con los gemelos.
Vuelve a la posición erguida impulsando la parte delantera del talón y extendiendo las rodillas y las caderas.

name: Barbell Floor Press With Chains
instructions: Adjust the j-hooks so they are at the appropriate height to rack the bar. For this exercise, drape the chains directly over the end of the bar, trying to keep the ends away from the plates.
Begin lying on the floor with your head near the end of a power rack. Keeping your shoulder blades pulled together, pull the bar off of the hooks.

Lower the bar towards the bottom of your chest or upper stomach, squeezing the bar and attempting to pull it apart as you do so. Ensure that you tuck your elbows throughout the movement. Lower the bar until your upper arm contacts the ground and pause, preventing any slamming or bouncing of the weight.
Press the bar back up as fast as you can, keeping the bar, your wrists, and elbows in line as you do so.

name.es: Press de suelo con barra y cadenas

es: Ajusta los ganchos en J para que estén a la altura adecuada para colocar la barra. Para este ejercicio, coloque las cadenas directamente sobre el extremo de la barra, tratando de mantener los extremos alejados de los discos.
Empieza tumbándote en el suelo con la cabeza cerca del final de un rack de potencia. Manteniendo los omóplatos juntos, saca la barra de los ganchos.

Baja la barra hacia la parte inferior de tu pecho o la parte superior del abdomen, apretando la barra e intentando separarla mientras lo haces. Asegúrate de doblar los codos durante todo el movimiento. Baja la barra hasta que la parte superior de tu brazo entre en contacto con el suelo y haga una pausa, evitando cualquier golpe o rebote del peso.
Empuja la barra hacia arriba lo más rápido que puedas, manteniendo la barra, las muñecas y los codos alineados mientras lo haces.

name: Palms Up Barbell Wrist Curls Over A Bench
instructions: Hold a barbell with both hands and your palms facing up; hands spaced about shoulder width.
Place your feet flat on the floor, at a distance that is slightly wider than shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.
Lower the bar as far as possible by extending the fingers. Allowing the bar to roll down the hands, catch the bar with the final joint in the fingers.
Now curl bar up as high as possible by closing your hands while exhaling. Hold the contraction at the top.

name.en: Palms Up Barbell Wrist Curls Over A Bench

en: Hold a barbell with both hands and your palms facing up; hands spaced about shoulder width.
Place your feet flat on the floor, at a distance that is slightly wider than shoulder width apart. This will be your starting position.
Lower the bar as far as possible by extending the fingers. Allowing the bar to roll down the hands, catch the bar with the final joint in the fingers.
Now curl bar up as high as possible by closing your hands while exhaling. Hold the contraction at the top.
es: Sujeta una barra con ambas manos y las palmas hacia arriba; manos espaciadas aproximadamente al ancho de los hombros.
Coloca los pies apoyados en el suelo, a una distancia ligeramente mayor que el ancho de los hombros. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Baja la barra lo máximo posible extendiendo los dedos. Dejando que la barra baje por las manos, agarre la barra con la articulación final con los dedos.
Ahora levante la barra lo más alto posible cerrando las manos mientras exhala. Mantén la contracción en la parte superior.

name: Decline Narrow Grip EZ Bar Skullcrushers
instructions: Grab an EZ barbell and lay on a decline bench with the bar pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Adopt a pronated (overhand) grip with your elbows tucked in. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the barbell down towards the back of your head. Be sure to keep your elbows tucked in and stretch your triceps as you go. When you get to within an inch of the bench, pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar along the same path to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Decline Press Ups or Overhead Tricep Extensions Rope.

en: Grab an EZ barbell and lay on a decline bench with the bar pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Adopt a pronated (overhand) grip with your elbows tucked in. This will be your starting position.

Inhale as you descend the barbell down towards the back of your head. Be sure to keep your elbows tucked in and stretch your triceps as you go. When you get to within an inch of the bench, pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar along the same path to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Decline Press Ups or Overhead Tricep Extensions Rope.
es: Coge una barra EZ y recuéstese en un banco declinado con la barra extendida hacia el techo. Adopte un agarre en pronación (sobre la cabeza) con los codos pegados al cuerpo. Esta será su posición inicial.

Inhala mientras desciendes la barra hacia la parte posterior de tu cabeza. Asegúrate de mantener los codos doblados y estirar los tríceps durante el movimiento. Cuando llegue a unos 2-3 cm del banco, haga una pausa por un segundo.
Luego, mientras exhalas, usa tus tríceps para devolver la barra por el mismo camino hasta la posición inicial. Tus brazos deben estar completamente extendidos una vez más y apuntando hacia el techo.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: flexiones declinadas o extensiones de tríceps sobre la cabeza con cuerda.

aliases: Decline Narrow Grip EZ Bar Skullcrushers

name: Narrow Grip EZ Bar Skullcrushers
instructions: Grab an EZ bar (overhand grip) and lay flat on a bench with the bar pressed straight up towards the ceiling. Keep your elbows as close together as you can to initiate maximum tricep activity. This will be your starting position.
Inhale as you descend the bar towards your forehead. Be sure to keep your upper arms stationary and your elbows tucked in. When you get to within an inch of your forehead pause for a second.
Then whilst exhaling, use your triceps to return the bar to the starting position. Your arms should be fully extended once again and pointing towards the ceiling.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press or Tricep Rope Extensions.

es: Agarra una barra EZ (agarre prono) y recuéstese sobre un banco con la barra extendida hacia el techo. Mantén los codos lo más juntos posible para iniciar la máxima actividad de los tríceps. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Inhala mientras desciendes la barra hacia tu frente. Asegúrate de mantener la parte superior de los brazos inmóviles y los codos pegados al cuerpo. Cuando llegue a unos 2-3 cm de su frente, haga una pausa por un segundo.
Luego, mientras exhalas, usa tus tríceps para devolver la barra a la posición inicial. Tus brazos deben estar completamente extendidos una vez más y apuntando hacia el techo.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Press de banca con barra con agarre estrecho o extensiones de cuerda para tríceps.

name: Wide Grip EZ Bar Curls
instructions: Grab an EZ bar with a shoulder width, underhand grip (palms facing forward). Allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor and adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the bar upward towards your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so and remember, only your forearms should move.
Continue in this fashion until your biceps are fully flexed and the bar is at shoulder height.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the bar along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Barbell Curls or Cable Curls.

name.es: Curl con barra EZ, agarre ancho

en: biceps

en: Grab an EZ bar with a shoulder width, underhand grip (palms facing forward). Allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor and adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the bar upward towards your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so and remember, only your forearms should move.
Continue in this fashion until your biceps are fully flexed and the bar is at shoulder height.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the bar along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Barbell Curls or Cable Curls.
es: Agarra una barra EZ con un ancho de hombros y agarre supino (con las palmas hacia adelante). Deja que sus brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo y adopte una posición de pie con una postura a la altura de los hombros y la espalda recta. Mantén los codos inmovilizados cerca del torso durante todo el ejercicio. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Mientras mantiene la parte superior de los brazos inmóviles, doble la barra hacia los hombros flexionando los bíceps. Exhala mientras lo haces y recuerda, sólo tus antebrazos deben moverse.
Continúa de esta manera hasta que sus bíceps estén completamente flexionados y la barra esté a la altura de los hombros.
Haz una pausa por un breve momento y luego regrese la barra por el mismo camino hacia la posición inicial mientras inhala.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Curl con barra de pie o Curl con cable.

name: Drop Snatch
instructions: This drill helps you learn the snatch. Begin by holding a light weight across the back of the shoulders. Your feet should be slightly wider than hip width apart with the feet turned out, the same position that you would perform a squat with.
Begin by dipping with the knees slightly, and popping back up to briefly unload the bar. Drive yourself underneath the bar, elevating it overhead as you descend into a full squat.
Return to a standing position.

en: This drill helps you learn the snatch. Begin by holding a light weight across the back of the shoulders. Your feet should be slightly wider than hip width apart with the feet turned out, the same position that you would perform a squat with.
Begin by dipping with the knees slightly, and popping back up to briefly unload the bar. Drive yourself underneath the bar, elevating it overhead as you descend into a full squat.
Return to a standing position.
es: Este ejercicio te ayuda a aprender el arranque. Empieza sosteniendo un peso liviano sobre la parte posterior de los hombros. Tus pies deben estar ligeramente más anchos que el ancho de las caderas con los pies hacia afuera, la misma posición con la que realizarías una sentadilla.

Empieza bajando ligeramente las rodillas y volviendo a subir para descargar brevemente la barra. Conduce por debajo de la barra, elevándola por encima de tu cabeza mientras desciendes hasta hacer una sentadilla completa.
Vuelve a una posición de pie.

name: Barbell Drag Curl
instructions: Grab a barbell with a supinated grip (palms facing forward) and get your elbows close to your torso and back. This will be your starting position.

As you exhale, curl the bar up while keeping the elbows to the back as you "Drag" the bar up by keeping it in contact with your torso.  Tip: As you can see, you will not be keeping the elbows pinned to your sides, but instead you will be bringing them back.  Also, do not lift your shoulders.
Slowly go back to the starting position as you keep the bar in contact with the torso at all times.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: You can use dumbbells or the smith machine.

en: biceps

es: antebrazos

en: Grab a barbell with a supinated grip (palms facing forward) and get your elbows close to your torso and back. This will be your starting position.

As you exhale, curl the bar up while keeping the elbows to the back as you "Drag" the bar up by keeping it in contact with your torso.  Tip: As you can see, you will not be keeping the elbows pinned to your sides, but instead you will be bringing them back.  Also, do not lift your shoulders.
Slowly go back to the starting position as you keep the bar in contact with the torso at all times.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: You can use dumbbells or the smith machine.
es: Agarra una barra con un agarre supinado (con las palmas hacia adelante) y acerque los codos al torso y la espalda. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Mientras exhala, doble la barra hacia arriba mientras mantiene los codos hacia atrás mientras "arrastra" la barra hacia arriba manteniéndola en contacto con su torso.  Consejo: Como puede ver, no mantendrá los codos inmovilizados a los costados, sino que los traerá hacia atrás.  Además, no levante los hombros.
Vuelve lentamente a la posición inicial mientras mantienes la barra en contacto con el torso en todo momento.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Puedes utilizar mancuernas o la máquina Smith.

name: Smith Machine Decline Bench Press
instructions: Lay on a decline bench in the smith machine. Take a medium grip (a grip which creates a 90 degree angle at your elbows half way through the movement) and rotate your wrists so that you take the weight of the bar, at this point it should be directly above your chest. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. This will be your starting position. Tip: Make sure you set the height of the safety bars just in case you get in trouble.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar gently touches your lower chest.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your pectoral muscles.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
When you are done, rotate your wrists so that the barbell is then securely racked up.
Caution: You should always have a spotter when attempting this movement. If a spotter is not available you should be conservative with the weight you are using.
Variations: Decline Barbell Bench Press or Decline Dumbbell Chest Press.

en: Lay on a decline bench in the smith machine. Take a medium grip (a grip which creates a 90 degree angle at your elbows half way through the movement) and rotate your wrists so that you take the weight of the bar, at this point it should be directly above your chest. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. This will be your starting position. Tip: Make sure you set the height of the safety bars just in case you get in trouble.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar gently touches your lower chest.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your pectoral muscles.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
When you are done, rotate your wrists so that the barbell is then securely racked up.
Caution: You should always have a spotter when attempting this movement. If a spotter is not available you should be conservative with the weight you are using.
Variations: Decline Barbell Bench Press or Decline Dumbbell Chest Press.
es: Acuéstese en un banco inclinado en la máquina Smith. Coge un agarre medio (un agarre que crea un ángulo de 90 grados en los codos a la mitad del movimiento) y gire las muñecas para soportar el peso de la barra; en este punto debe estar directamente encima de tu pecho. Mantén los codos bloqueados en este punto por seguridad. Esta será tu posición inicial. Consejo: asegúrate de establecer la altura de los seguros en caso de que tenga problemas.
Mientras inhala, comience a bajar la barra doblando los codos hasta que la barra toque suavemente la parte inferior del pecho.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala y presiona la barra hacia la posición inicial contrayendo los músculos pectorales.
Repite el movimiento durante la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Cuando haya terminado, gire las muñecas para que la barra quede fijada de forma segura.
Precaución: siempre debe tener un observador cuando intente este movimiento. Si no hay un observador disponible, debe ser conservador con el peso que está utilizando.
Variaciones: Press de banca declinado con barra o Press de pecho declinado con mancuernas.

Variations: Smith Machine Decline Press or Decline Dumbbell Chest Press.

name: Deadlift from a Deficit
instructions: Begin by having a platform or weight plates that you can stand on, usually 1-3 inches in height. Approach the bar so that it is centered over your feet. You feet should be about hip width apart. Bend at the hip to grip the bar at shoulder width, allowing your shoulder blades to protract. Typically, you would use an overhand grip or an over/under grip on heavier sets.

With your feet, and your grip set, take a big breath and then lower your hips and bend the knees until your shins contact the bar. Look forward with your head, keep your chest up and your back arched, and begin driving through the heels to move the weight upward. After the bar passes the knees, aggressively pull the bar back, pulling your shoulder blades together as you drive your hips forward into the bar.
Lower the bar by bending at the hips and guiding it to the floor.

With your feet, and your grip set, take a big breath and then lower your hips and bend the knees until your shins contact the bar. After the bar passes the knees, aggressively pull the bar back, pulling your shoulder blades together as you drive your hips forward into the bar.

es: Empieza por tener una plataforma o discos de pesas sobre los que pueda pararse, generalmente de 2,5 a 7,5 cm de altura. Acércate a la barra para que quede centrada sobre tus pies. Tus pies deben estar separados aproximadamente al ancho de las caderas. Doble la cadera para agarrar la barra al ancho de los hombros, permitiendo que los omóplatos se prolonguen. Por lo general, usarías un agarre por encima o por debajo en series más pesadas.

Con los pies y el agarre firme, respire hondo y luego baje las caderas y doble las rodillas hasta que las espinillas entren en contacto con la barra. Mire hacia adelante con la cabeza, mantenga el pecho erguido y la espalda arqueada, y comience a impulsar con los talones para mover el peso hacia arriba. Después de que la barra pase las rodillas, tire agresivamente de la barra hacia atrás, juntando los omóplatos mientras impulsa las caderas hacia adelante hacia la barra.
Baja la barra doblándola por las caderas y guiándola hacia el suelo.

name: Narrow Grip Barbell Preacher Curls
instructions: Grab a barbell with an underhand grip and take a seat on a preacher curl machine. Place your upper arms over the pad of the machine with your palms facing forward. Set the height of the seat so that your arm pits are snug on top of the pad and have your arms extended. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and contract your biceps to lift the barbell up towards your shoulders. Keep your triceps on the pad and squeeze your biceps until they are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the barbell along the same path back into its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Cable Curls or Two-Arm Dumbbell Preacher Curl.

name.es: Curl predicador con barra, agarre estrecho

en: Grab a barbell with an underhand grip and take a seat on a preacher curl machine. Place your upper arms over the pad of the machine with your palms facing forward. Set the height of the seat so that your arm pits are snug on top of the pad and have your arms extended. This will be your starting position.

Exhale and contract your biceps to lift the barbell up towards your shoulders. Keep your triceps on the pad and squeeze your biceps until they are fully contracted and the barbell is at shoulder height.
After a brief pause, inhale and slowly lower the barbell along the same path back into its starting position.

Variations: Cable Curls or Two-Arm Dumbbell Preacher Curl.
es: Agarra una barra con agarre supino y siéntese en una máquina de curl predicador. Coloca la parte superior de sus brazos sobre la almohadilla de la máquina con las palmas hacia adelante. Ajusta la altura del asiento de modo que las axilas queden ajustadas sobre la almohadilla y los brazos extendidos. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Exhala y contrae los bíceps para levantar la barra hacia los hombros. Mantén los tríceps sobre la almohadilla y aprieta los bíceps hasta que estén completamente contraídos y la barra quede a la altura de los hombros.
Después de una breve pausa, inhala y baja lentamente la barra por el mismo camino hasta su posición inicial.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Curl con cable o Curl predicador con mancuernas de dos brazos.

name: Close-Grip Standing Barbell Curl
instructions: Grab a barbell with a narrow, underhand grip (palms facing forward), and allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor. Adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the barbell upward toward your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so and remember, only your forearms should move.
Continue in this fashion until your biceps are fully flexed and the barbell is at shoulder height.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the barbell along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Narrow Grip EZ Bar Curls or Cable Curls.

Pause for a brief moment and then return the barbell along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.

Variations: Narrow Grip EZ Bar Curls or Cable Curls.
es: Agarra una barra con un agarre estrecho y por debajo (con las palmas hacia adelante) y deje que sus brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo. Adopte una posición de pie con una postura a la altura de los hombros y la espalda recta. Mantén los codos inmovilizados cerca del torso durante todo el ejercicio. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Mientras mantiene la parte superior de los brazos inmóviles, doble la barra hacia los hombros flexionando los bíceps. Exhala mientras lo haces y recuerda, sólo tus antebrazos deben moverse.
Continúa de esta manera hasta que sus bíceps estén completamente flexionados y la barra esté a la altura de los hombros.
Haz una pausa por un breve momento y luego regrese la barra por el mismo camino hacia la posición inicial mientras inhala.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Curl con barra EZ de agarre estrecho o Curl con cable.

name: Narrow Grip EZ Bar Curls
instructions: Grab an EZ bar with a narrow, underhand grip (palms facing forward). Allow your arms the hang perpendicular to the floor and adopt a standing position with a shoulder width stance and a straight back. Keep your elbows pinned in close to your torso for the duration of the exercise. This will be your starting position.
Whilst keeping your upper arms stationary, curl the bar upward towards your shoulders by flexing your biceps. Exhale as you do so and remember, only your forearms should move.
Continue in this fashion until your biceps are fully flexed and the bar is at shoulder height.
Pause for a brief moment and then return the bar along the same path toward the starting position as you inhale.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Curls or Cable Curls.

name: Narrow Grip EZ Bar Press
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with an EZ bar loaded to an appropriate weight.
Using a narrow grip lift the bar and hold it straight over your torso with your elbows in. The arms should be perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Now lower the bar down to your lower chest as you breathe in. Keep the elbows in as you perform this movement.
Using the triceps to push the bar back up, press it back to the starting position by extending the elbows as you exhale.
Repeat.

es: Acuéstese en un banco plano con una barra EZ cargada con un peso adecuado.
Usando un agarre estrecho, levante la barra y manténgala recta sobre su torso con los codos pegados. Los brazos deben estar perpendiculares al suelo. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Ahora baje la barra hasta la parte inferior del pecho mientras inhala. Mantén los codos pegados mientras realiza este movimiento.
Usando los tríceps para empujar la barra hacia arriba, empújela hacia la posición inicial extendiendo los codos mientras exhala.
Repetir.

name: Close-Grip EZ-Bar Curl with Band
instructions: Attach a band to each end of the bar. Take the bar, placing a foot on the middle of the band. Stand upright with a narrow, supinated grip on the EZ bar. The elbows should be close to the torso. This will be your starting position.
While keeping the upper arms in place, flex the elbows to execute the curl. Exhale as the weight is lifted.
Continue the movement until your biceps are fully contracted and the bar is at shoulder level. Hold the contracted position for a second and squeeze the biceps hard.
Slowly begin to bring the bar back to its starting position as you breathe in.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.

Mientras mantiene la parte superior de los brazos en su lugar, flexione los codos para ejecutar el curl. Exhala mientras levantas el peso.
Continúa el movimiento hasta que tus bíceps estén completamente contraídos y la barra esté a la altura de los hombros. Mantén la posición contraída durante un segundo y aprieta con fuerza el bíceps.
Empieza lentamente a llevar la barra a su posición inicial mientras inhala.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.

name: Narrow Grip Barbell Bench Press
instructions: Lay flat on a bench with a barbell in the rack above you. Take a close grip (6-12 inches between your index fingers) and lift the bar off the rack so that it is directly above your chest. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. This will be your starting position.

Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar gently touches the middle of your chest. Your elbows should remain close together and should brush along the sides of your torso as you go.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back along the same path into the starting position by contracting your triceps and pectoral muscles.
Repeat the movement for the recommended amount of repetitions.
When you are done, place the bar back in the rack.
Caution: You should always have a spotter when attempting this movement. If a spotter is not available you should be conservative with the weight you are using.
Variations: Smith Machine Close Grip Bench Press or Tricep Dips.

es: Acuéstese en un banco con una barra en el rack encima de ti. Agarra con fuerza (de 6 a 30 cm entre sus dedos índices) y levante la barra del rack para que quede directamente sobre tu pecho. Mantén los codos bloqueados en este punto por seguridad. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Mientras inhala, comience a bajar la barra doblando los codos hasta que la barra toque suavemente la mitad de tu pecho. Tus codos deben permanecer juntos y deben rozar los costados de tu torso durante el movimientos.
Después de una breve pausa, exhale y presione la barra hacia atrás a lo largo del mismo camino hasta la posición inicial contrayendo los tríceps y los músculos pectorales.
Repite el movimiento durante la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Cuando haya terminado, coloque la barra nuevamente en el rack.
Precaución: siempre debe tener un observador cuando intente este movimiento. Si no hay un observador disponible, debe ser conservador con el peso que está utilizando.
Variaciones: Press de banca con agarre cerrado en máquina Smith o fondos de tríceps.

name: Barbell Clean Pull
instructions: Grab a barbell with a slightly wider than shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bar is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the barbell. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bar gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the barbell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the barbell further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.
After you have achieved full extension you need to bring the bar back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Cleans or Barbell Cleans.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bar gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the barbell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the barbell further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.
After you have achieved full extension you need to bring the bar back along the same path into the starting position.

es: Agarra una barra con un agarre ligeramente más ancho que el ancho de los hombros sobre la mano. Doble las rodillas y las caderas (más las caderas que las rodillas), manteniendo la espalda recta y permitiendo que los brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo hasta que la barra esté justo debajo de las rodillas. En este punto, tu pecho, hombros y cabeza deben mirar hacia adelante. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Tu primer movimiento será impulsar los talones y los cuádriceps para comenzar a elevar la barra. Tu espalda debe permanecer recta y tus brazos aún deben estar perpendiculares al suelo en este punto.

Luego viene la aceleración de la pelvis; debes impulsar la pelvis hacia adelante cuando la barra llegue a la mitad del muslo. Esto, junto con la extensión a través de las articulaciones de la rodilla y el tobillo, impulsará la barra hacia arriba.

Una vez que hayas empujado las caderas hacia adelante, es hora de encogerte de hombros lo más fuerte que puedas para levantar más la barra. Expanda los codos hacia arriba y hacia afuera hasta que tu cuerpo esté completamente extendido.
Una vez que haya alcanzado la extensión completa, deberá llevar la barra de regreso por el mismo camino hasta la posición inicial.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: cargadas con mancuernas o cargadas con barra.

name: Cleans From Blocks
instructions: With a barbell on boxes or stands of the desired height, take an overhand or hook grip just outside the legs. Lower your hips with the weight focused on the heels, back straight, head facing forward, chest up, with your shoulders just in front of the bar. This will be your starting position.
Begin the first pull by driving through the heels, extending your knees. Your back angle should stay the same, and your arms should remain straight with the elbows pointed out.

Next comes the second pull, the main source of acceleration for the clean. As the bar approaches the mid-thigh position, begin extending through the hips. In a jumping motion, accelerate by extending the hips, knees, and ankles, using speed to move the bar upward. There should be no need to actively pull through the arms to accelerate the weight. At the end of the second pull, the body should be fully extended, leaning slightly back, with the arms still extended.

As full extension is achieved, transition into the receiving position by aggressively shrugging and flexing the arms with the elbows up and out. Aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your elbows under the bar as you do so. Receive the bar in a front squat position, the depth of which is dependent upon the height of the bar at the end of the third pull. The bar should be racked onto the protracted shoulders, lightly touching the throat with the hands relaxed. Continue to descend to the bottom squat position, which will help in the recovery.
Immediately recover by driving through the heels, keeping the torso upright and elbows up. Continue until you have risen to a standing position. Return the weight to the boxes for the next rep.

As full extension is achieved, transition into the receiving position by aggressively shrugging and flexing the arms with the elbows up and out. Aggressively pull yourself down, rotating your elbows under the bar as you do so. Receive the bar in a front squat position, the depth of which is dependent upon the height of the bar at the end of the third pull. The bar should be racked onto the protracted shoulders, lightly touching the throat with the hands relaxed. Continue to descend to the bottom squat position, which will help in the recovery.
Immediately recover by driving through the heels, keeping the torso upright and elbows up. Continue until you have risen to a standing position. Return the weight to the boxes for the next rep.
es: Con una barra sobre cajas o soportes de la altura deseada, tome un agarre prono o con gancho justo fuera de las piernas. Baja las caderas con el peso centrado en los talones, la espalda recta, la cabeza mirando hacia adelante, el pecho hacia arriba y los hombros justo delante de la barra. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Empieza el primer tirón impulsando los talones y extendiendo las rodillas. El ángulo de la espalda debe permanecer igual y los brazos deben permanecer rectos con los codos apuntando.

Luego viene el segundo tirón, la principal fuente de aceleración del clean. A medida que la barra se acerca a la posición de la mitad del muslo, comience a extenderse a través de las caderas. En un movimiento de salto, acelere extendiendo las caderas, las rodillas y los tobillos, usando la velocidad para mover la barra hacia arriba. No debería ser necesario tirar activamente de los brazos para acelerar el peso. Al final del segundo tirón, el cuerpo debe estar completamente extendido, ligeramente inclinado hacia atrás, con los brazos aún extendidos.

Una vez que se logre la extensión completa, haga la transición a la posición de recepción encogiéndose de hombros y flexionando agresivamente los brazos con los codos hacia arriba y hacia afuera. Tira hacia abajo agresivamente, girando los codos debajo de la barra mientras lo haces. Reciba la barra en posición de sentadilla frontal, cuya profundidad depende de la altura de la barra al final del tercer tirón. La barra debe colocarse sobre los hombros extendidos, tocando ligeramente la garganta con las manos relajadas. Continúa descendiendo hasta la posición de sentadilla inferior, lo que ayudará en la recuperación.

Recupérese inmediatamente impulsando los talones, manteniendo el torso erguido y los codos hacia arriba. Continúa hasta que se haya puesto de pie. Devuelve el peso a las cajas para la siguiente repetición.

name: Barbell Clean & Press
instructions: Grab a barbell with a slightly wider than shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bar is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the barbell. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bar gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the barbell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the barbell further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bar and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to press the barbell above your head until your elbows are locked out. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.
Descend the barbell back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure to keep the bar close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Barbell Clean & Jerk or Barbell Cleans.

es: Agarra una barra con un agarre ligeramente más ancho que el ancho de los hombros sobre la mano. Doble las rodillas y las caderas (más las caderas que las rodillas), manteniendo la espalda recta y permitiendo que los brazos cuelguen perpendiculares al suelo hasta que la barra esté justo debajo de las rodillas. En este punto, tu pecho, hombros y cabeza deben mirar hacia adelante. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Tu primer movimiento será impulsar los talones y los cuádriceps para comenzar a elevar la barra. Tu espalda debe permanecer recta y tus brazos aún deben estar perpendiculares al suelo en este punto.

Luego viene la aceleración de la pelvis; debes impulsar la pelvis hacia adelante cuando la barra llegue a la mitad del muslo. Esto, junto con la extensión a través de las articulaciones de la rodilla y el tobillo, impulsará la barra hacia arriba.

Una vez que hayas empujado las caderas hacia adelante, es hora de encogerte de hombros lo más fuerte que puedas para levantar más la barra. Expanda los codos hacia arriba y hacia afuera hasta que tu cuerpo esté completamente extendido.

Cuando esté en extensión completa, agáchese agresivamente debajo de la barra y gire los codos hacia abajo y hacia adentro para que pueda soportar la carga sobre los deltoides frontales (hombros) en una posición de sentadilla frontal. Continúa en cuclillas hasta que se sienta cómodo impulsando hacia arriba los talones, los cuádriceps y los glúteos.
Continúa conduciendo hacia arriba hasta que esté de pie.

Una vez que esté de pie, doble las rodillas para generar energía para presionar la barra sobre su cabeza hasta que sus codos estén bloqueados. Consejo: Mantener la espalda recta en todo momento es primordial.
Desciende la barra nuevamente a la posición inicial invirtiendo este movimiento, asegurándote de mantener la barra cerca de tu cuerpo en todo momento y manteniendo una buena postura.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Barbell Clean & Jerk o Barbell Cleans.

name: Barbell Clean & Jerk
instructions: Grab a barbell with a slightly wider than shoulder width over hand grip. Bend at your knees and hips (more so with your hips than knees), maintaining a straight back and allowing your arms to hang perpendicular to the floor until the bar is just below your knees. Your chest, shoulders and head should be facing forward at this point. This will be your starting position.

Your first movement will be to drive through your heels and quads in order to start elevating the barbell. Your back should remain straight and your arms should still be perpendicular to the floor at this point.

Next comes the acceleration from your pelvis, you need to drive your pelvis forward when the bar gets to your mid thigh. This coupled with the extension through your knee and ankle joints, will propell the barbell upward.
Once you have thrusted your hips forward, it is time to shrug as hard as you can in order to lift the barbell further. Flare your elbows up and outward until your body is at full extension.

When at full extension, aggressively squat under the bar and rotate your elbows under and in so that you can take the load on your front deltoids (shoulders) in a front squat position. Continue to squat until you are comfortable to drive upward through your heels, quads and glutes.
Keep driving upward until you are in a standing position.

Once you are in a standing position, bend at your knees to generate power to jump into a split stance (top of a lunge). At the same time as powering the body into a jump, aggressively press the barbell above your head until your elbows are locked out.
Step forward with your rear leg so that you are now positioned under the bar. Tip: Maintaining a straight back throughout is paramount.

Either Drop the bar in a controlled manner if you are on a lifting platform, being careful not to hurt yourself or descend the barbell back into the starting position by reversing this movement, making sure you keep the bar close to your body throughout and maintaining good posture.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Kettlebell Clean And Jerk or Barbell Clean And Press.

Una vez que hayas empujado las caderas hacia adelante, es hora de encogerte de hombros lo más fuerte que puedas para levantar más la barra. Expanda los codos hacia arriba y hacia afuera hasta que tu cuerpo esté completamente extendido.

Deja caer la barra de manera controlada si está en una plataforma de elevación, teniendo cuidado de no lastimarse o baje la barra nuevamente a la posición inicial invirtiendo este movimiento, asegurándose de mantener la barra cerca de tu cuerpo en todo momento y manteniendo una buena postura.

Variaciones: cargada y sacudida con pesas rusas o cargada y press con barra.

name: Chain Press
instructions: Begin by connecting the chains to the cable handle attachments. Position yourself on the flat bench in the same position as for a dumbbell press. Your wrists should be pronated and arms perpendicular to the floor. This will be your starting position.
Lower the chains by flexing the elbows, unloading some of the chain onto the floor.
Continue until your elbow forms a 90-degree angle, and then reverse the motion by extending through the elbow to lockout.

es: Empieza conectando las cadenas a los accesorios del mango del cable. Colóquese en el banco plano en la misma posición que para el press con mancuernas. Tus muñecas deben estar en pronación y los brazos perpendiculares al suelo. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Bajar las cadenas flexionando los codos, descargando parte de la cadena al suelo.
Continúa hasta que su codo forme un ángulo de 90 grados y luego invierta el movimiento extendiéndolo a través del codo hasta bloquear.

name: Chained Deadlifts
instructions: You can attach the chains to the sleeves of the bar, or just drape the middle over the bar so there is a greater weight increase as you lift.

Approach the bar so that it is centered over your feet. You feet should be about hip width apart. Bend at the hip to grip the bar at shoulder width, allowing your shoulder blades to protract. Typically, you would use an overhand grip or an over/under grip on heavier sets. With your feet, and your grip set, take a big breath and then lower your hips and bend the knees until your shins contact the bar.

Look forward with your head, keep your chest up and your back arched, and begin driving through the heels to move the weight upward. After the bar passes the knees, aggressively pull the bar back, pulling your shoulder blades together as you drive your hips forward into the bar.
Lower the bar by bending at the hips and guiding it to the floor.

es: Puede sujetar las cadenas a las mangas de la barra o simplemente colocar el centro sobre la barra para que haya un mayor aumento de peso a medida que levanta.

Acércate a la barra para que quede centrada sobre tus pies. Tus pies deben estar separados aproximadamente al ancho de las caderas. Doble la cadera para agarrar la barra al ancho de los hombros, permitiendo que los omóplatos se prolonguen. Por lo general, usarías un agarre por encima o por debajo en series más pesadas. Con los pies y el agarre firme, respire hondo y luego baje las caderas y doble las rodillas hasta que las espinillas entren en contacto con la barra.

Mire hacia adelante con la cabeza, mantenga el pecho erguido y la espalda arqueada, y comience a impulsar con los talones para mover el peso hacia arriba. Después de que la barra pase las rodillas, tire agresivamente de la barra hacia atrás, juntando los omóplatos mientras impulsa las caderas hacia adelante hacia la barra.
Baja la barra doblándola por las caderas y guiándola hacia el suelo.

name: Bradford:Rocky Presses
instructions: Grab a barbell and adopt a shoulder width, over hand grip. Lift the bar above your head so that it is directly above your shoulders. Keep your elbows locked at this point for safety. Maintain a straight back throughout this movement. This will be your starting position.
Whilst inhaling, start to lower the bar by bending at your elbows until the bar is below your chin.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Immediately, inhale and lower the barbell down behind your head by bending and flaring your elbows out wide. Continue to lower the barbell until you are an inch or so away from your shoulders.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the bar back into the starting position by contracting your deltoid muscles (shoulders).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Those with back problems, may find a seated version more comfortable.
Variations: Seated Shoulder Press (Barbell) or Smith Machine Shoulder Press.

es: Agarra una barra y adopte un agarre a la altura de los hombros, sobre la mano. Levante la barra por encima de su cabeza para que quede directamente por encima de sus hombros. Mantén los codos bloqueados en este punto por seguridad. Mantén la espalda recta durante todo este movimiento. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Mientras inhala, comience a bajar la barra doblando los codos hasta que la barra esté debajo de la barbilla.
Después de una breve pausa, exhale y presione la barra hacia la posición inicial contrayendo los músculos deltoides (hombros).
Inmediatamente, inhale y baje la barra detrás de su cabeza doblando y abriendo los codos. Continúa bajando la barra hasta que esté aproximadamente a unos 2-3 cm de sus hombros.
Después de una breve pausa, exhale y presione la barra hacia la posición inicial contrayendo los músculos deltoides (hombros).
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Precaución: Las personas con problemas de espalda pueden encontrar más cómoda la versión sentada.
Variaciones: Press de hombros sentado (con barra) o Press de hombros con máquina Smith.

name: Single Arm Landmine Bent Over Rows
instructions: Load up a barbell with the desired amount of weight, place it into a stirrup or in the corner of the room and take your stance either side of the bar. Grip the bar close to the weight plate(s) with one hand and bend at your knees and waist (more so at your waist) to get your body in position. Your arm should be hanging perpendicular to the floor at this point. This is your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout.
Exhale and squeeze the muscles in your back to bring the barbell in towards your torso. Bend at the elbow to allow the movement but try not to take any weight through your forearms/bicep.
Once the barbell is as close to your body as it can be or the muscles in your back are as tight as they can be, inhale and return the barbell along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Caution: This exercise may not be suitable for people that suffer from any back pain/injuries.
Variations: Dumbbell Row or Seated One-Arm Cable Pulley Rows.

Your arm should be hanging perpendicular to the floor at this point. This is your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout.
Exhale and squeeze the muscles in your back to bring the barbell in towards your torso. Bend at the elbow to allow the movement but try not to take any weight through your forearms/bicep.
Once the barbell is as close to your body as it can be or the muscles in your back are as tight as they can be, inhale and return the barbell along the same path back into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions and swap sides.
Caution: This exercise may not be suitable for people that suffer from any back pain/injuries.
Variations: Dumbbell Row or Seated One-Arm Cable Pulley Rows.
es: Cargue una barra con la cantidad de peso deseada, colóquela en un estribo o en la esquina de la habitación y colóquese a ambos lados de la barra. Sujeta la barra cerca de los discos de pesas con una mano y doble las rodillas y la cintura (más aún en la cintura) para colocar el cuerpo en posición. En este punto, tu brazo debe colgar perpendicular al suelo. Esta es tu posición inicial. Consejo: mantenga la espalda recta en todo momento.
Exhala y aprieta los músculos de la espalda para acercar la barra hacia el torso. Doble el codo para permitir el movimiento, pero trate de no pasar ningún peso por los antebrazos o bíceps.
Una vez que la barra esté lo más cerca posible de tu cuerpo o los músculos de su espalda estén lo más tensos posible, inhale y regrese la barra por el mismo camino hasta la posición inicial.
Repite la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones y cambie de lado.
Precaución: Este ejercicio puede no ser adecuado para personas que sufren algún dolor o lesión en la espalda.
Variaciones: remo con mancuernas o remo sentado en polea con cable a un brazo.

aliases: Bent Over One-Arm Long Bar Row
Single Arm Landmine Bent Over Rows

name: Barbell Anti Gravity Press
instructions: Grab a barbell and lean into an incline bench, making sure your head is off the end. Take hold of the barbell with an overhand grip and hold it close to the bench at shoulder height, parallel to the floor. Have your elbows bent at a 90 degree angle and flared out wide. This is you starting position.

To initiate the movement, exhale and press the bar away from your shoulders maintaining its height off the floor. Continue to press away from your body until your arms are at full extension (keeping a slight bend at your elbows).
After a brief pause, inhale and return the barbell along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Alternating Front Raises or Arnold Dumbbell Press.

After a brief pause, inhale and return the barbell along the same path back to its starting position.

es: Toma una barra e inclínate en un banco inclinado, asegurándote de que tu cabeza esté fuera del extremo. Sujeta la barra con un agarre prono y manténgala cerca del banco a la altura de los hombros, paralela al suelo. Mantén los codos doblados en un ángulo de 90 grados y ensanchados. Esta es tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, exhale y presione la barra lejos de sus hombros manteniendo su altura del suelo. Continúa presionando lejos de tu cuerpo hasta que sus brazos estén completamente extendidos (manteniendo una ligera flexión en los codos).
Después de una breve pausa, inhala y regresa la barra por el mismo camino hasta su posición inicial.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Elevaciones frontales alternas o Press Arnold con mancuernas.

name: Biceps Curl
instructions: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance with your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor, by your sides.This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbells both up and outward whilst rotating your palms to face the ceiling.
Continue on an upward motion until your biceps are optimally contracted.
After this, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Concentration Curls or Concentration Curls.

en: biceps
es: bíceps

en: Grab a dumbbell in each hand and adopt a shoulder width stance with your arms hanging perpendicular to the floor, by your sides.This will be your starting position.
To initiate the movement, exhale and curl the dumbbells both up and outward whilst rotating your palms to face the ceiling.
Continue on an upward motion until your biceps are optimally contracted.
After this, inhale and return the dumbbells along the same path back to their starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Standing Concentration Curls or Concentration Curls.
es: Agarra una mancuerna en cada mano y adopta una postura a la altura de los hombros con los brazos colgando perpendiculares al suelo, a los lados. Esta será tu posición inicial.
Para iniciar el movimiento, exhale y doble las mancuernas hacia arriba y hacia afuera mientras gira las palmas de las manos hacia el techo.
Continúa con un movimiento ascendente hasta que sus bíceps estén óptimamente contraídos.
Después de esto, inhala y regresa las mancuernas por el mismo camino a su posición inicial.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Curls de concentración de pie o Curls de concentración.

name: Landmine Bent Over Two Arm Rows
instructions: Put weight on one of the ends of an Olympic barbell. Make sure that you either place the other end of the barbell in the corner of two walls; or put a heavy object  on the ground so the barbell cannot slide backward.
Bend forward until your torso is as close to parallel with the floor as you can and keep your knees slightly bent.
Now grab the bar with both arms just behind the plates on the side where the weight was placed and put your other hand on your knee. This will be your starting position.

Pull the bar straight up with your elbows in (to maximize back stimulation) until the plates touch your lower chest. Squeeze the back muscles as you lift the weight up and hold for a second at the top of the movement.  Breathe out as you lift the weight. Tip: Use a stirrup or double handle cable attachment by hooking it under the end of the bar.

Slowly lower the bar to the starting position getting a nice stretch on the lats. Tip: Do not let the plates touch the floor.  To ensure the best range of motion, I recommend using small plates (25-lb ones) as opposed to larger plates (like 35-45lb ones).
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: You can perform this exercise using a low pulley or T-bar row machine.

es: Coloca peso sobre uno de los extremos de una barra olímpica. Asegúrate de colocar el otro extremo de la barra en la esquina de dos paredes; o coloque un objeto pesado en el suelo para que la barra no pueda deslizarse hacia atrás.
Inclínese hacia adelante hasta que su torso esté lo más paralelo posible al suelo y mantenga las rodillas ligeramente dobladas.
Ahora agarra la barra con ambos brazos justo detrás de los discos del lado donde se colocó el peso y coloca la otra mano sobre tu rodilla. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Tire de la barra hacia arriba con los codos pegados (para maximizar la estimulación de la espalda) hasta que los discos toquen la parte inferior del pecho. Apriete los músculos de la espalda mientras levanta el peso y manténgalo así durante un segundo en la parte superior del movimiento.  Exhale mientras levanta el peso. Consejo: Utilice un estribo o un accesorio de cable de doble mango enganchándolo debajo del extremo de la barra.

Baja lentamente la barra hasta la posición inicial haciendo un buen estiramiento de los dorsales. Consejo: No dejes que los discos toquen el suelo.  Para garantizar el mejor rango de movimiento, recomiendo usar discos pequeños (de 10-12 kg) en lugar de discos más grandes (como las de 16 a 20 kg).
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Puedes realizar este ejercicio utilizando una máquina de remo con polea baja o con barra en T.

aliases: Bent Over Two-Arm Long Bar Row
Landmine Bent Over Two Arm Rows

name: Bent Arm Barbell Pullover
instructions: Grab a barbell and lay on a flat bench (face up). Have your arms extended straight up towards the ceiling with your palms facing away from you (at shoulders width). Now Bend your elbows so they are at 90 degrees with the barbell is just above your forehead. This will be your starting position.

To initiate the movement, inhale and slowly bring your elbows towards your ears, lowering the barbell down towards the floor. Tip: Maintain the 90 degree bend at your elbows throughout. The barbell should now be below your head.
After a brief pause, exhale and return the barbell along the same path back to its starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Bent-Arm Dumbbell Pullover or Cable Pullover.

name.es: Pullover con barra y brazos flexionados

es: Coge una barra y recuéstate en un banco plano (boca arriba). Mantén los brazos extendidos hacia el techo con las palmas hacia afuera (al ancho de los hombros). Ahora doble los codos para que queden a 90 grados con la barra justo encima de la frente. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Para iniciar el movimiento, inhala y lleva lentamente los codos hacia las orejas, bajando la barra hacia el suelo. Consejo: Mantén la curvatura de 90 grados en los codos en todo momento. La barra ahora debería estar debajo de tu cabeza.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala y regresa la barra por el mismo camino hasta su posición inicial.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Pullover con mancuernas con brazo doblado o Pullover con cable.

name: Belt Squats
instructions: Grab a belt and strap the desired amount of weight to it. Next, place the belt around your waist and make sure it is securely fastened. Adopt a shoulder width stance with your toes pointing slightly outward. This is your starting position. Tip: Throughout the movement you should keep your head up and maintain a straight back.

Descend slowly by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel with the floor. Keep a straight back so that your posture is not compromised. Tip: When at the bottom of the movement your knees should be behind your toes.

Once you have hit the bottom of the movement, pause for a second before pushing your torso back into the starting position through your heels. As you descend inhale and as you ascend, exhale. You should now be at the starting position, ready to go again.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: Always be aware of your posture with this exercise as if performed incorrectly, could cause injury.
Variations: Plie Dumbbell Squats or Leg Press (Wide Stance).

es: Coge un cinturón y ata a él la cantidad de peso deseada. Luego, coloque el cinturón alrededor de su cintura y asegúrate de que esté bien sujeto. Adopte una postura a la altura de los hombros con los dedos de los pies apuntando ligeramente hacia afuera. Esta es tu posición inicial. Consejo: Durante todo el movimiento debes mantener la cabeza erguida y mantener la espalda recta.

Desciende lentamente doblando las rodillas hasta que tus muslos queden paralelos al suelo. Mantén la espalda recta para que su postura no se vea comprometida. Consejo: cuando estés en la parte inferior del movimiento, tus rodillas deben estar detrás de los dedos de los pies.

Una vez que hayas llegado al final del movimiento, haz una pausa por un segundo antes de empujar el torso hacia la posición inicial a través de los talones. Al descender, inhala y al ascender, exhala. Ahora deberías estar en la posición inicial, listo para comenzar de nuevo.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Precaución: Esté siempre consciente de su postura al realizar este ejercicio, ya que, si se realiza incorrectamente, podría causar lesiones.
Variaciones: Sentadillas Plie con mancuernas o Press de piernas (postura amplia).

name: Standing Barbell Shoulder Press Behind The Head
instructions: Grab a barbell with a wider than shoulder width overhand grip and take a seat on a flat bench. Lift the barbell above your head towards the ceiling so that your elbows are locked out and your palms are facing forward. This is your starting position.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell down behind your head making sure you are nowhere near your neck! Continue until your elbows are at 90 degrees or slightly past.
After a brief pause, exhale and press the barbell back along the same path into the starting position.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Smith Machine Overhead Shoulder Press or Standing Dumbbell Press.

es: Agarra una barra con un agarre por encima del ancho de los hombros y siéntese en un banco plano. Levante la barra por encima de su cabeza hacia el techo de modo que sus codos queden bloqueados y sus palmas miren hacia adelante. Esta es tu posición inicial.
¡Inhala y baja lentamente la barra detrás de tu cabeza asegurándote de no estar cerca de tu cuello! Continúa hasta que sus codos estén a 90 grados o ligeramente más allá.
Después de una breve pausa, exhala y empuja la barra hacia atrás por el mismo camino hasta la posición inicial.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: Press de hombros con máquina Smith o Press de pie con mancuernas.

aliases: Standing Barbell Shoulder Press Behind The Head

name: Barbell Walking Lunges
instructions: Grab and place a barbell across the back of your shoulders. Stand with a straight back, hands placed on the bar for stability in a shoulder width stance. This will be your starting position. Tip: Maintain a straight back throughout.

Take a step forward (slightly larger than a normal step) and as you land, drop your body down so that you create two right angles at your knees. Inhale as you do so whilst maintaining a straight torso.
Make sure that you keep your front knee over your heel and not over your toes. Your front shin should now be perpendicular to the floor.
As you exhale, push off the floor with your rear foot to return your body into a standing position.
Alternate legs as you go (as you walk forwards) and repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Bodyweight Walking Lunges or Dumbbell Walking Lunges.

es: Agarra y coloque una barra en la parte posterior de sus hombros. Párese con la espalda recta y las manos colocadas en la barra para mayor estabilidad en una postura a la altura de los hombros. Esta será tu posición inicial. Consejo: mantenga la espalda recta en todo momento.

Da un paso hacia adelante (un poco más grande que un paso normal) y, cuando aterrices, deja caer el cuerpo para crear dos ángulos rectos en las rodillas. Inhala mientras lo haces manteniendo el torso recto.
Asegúrate de mantener la rodilla delantera sobre el talón y no sobre los dedos de los pies. Tu espinilla delantera ahora debería estar perpendicular al suelo.
Mientras exhala, empuje el suelo con el pie trasero para que el cuerpo vuelva a la posición de pie.
Alterne las piernas durante el movimiento (mientras camina hacia adelante) y repita hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Variaciones: estocadas caminando con peso corporal o estocadas caminando con mancuernas.

name: Barbell Stiff Legged Deadlifts
instructions: Grab a loaded barbell and adopt a standing position with your feet shoulder width apart. Have your arms in front of you but still perpendicular to the floor (overhand grip, palms facing behind you). Slightly bend at your knees and maintain a straight back throughout. This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your knees stationary, inhale as you lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the barbell close to you as you go. Keep lowering the barbell towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.

After a brief pause bring your torso upright again by pushing your hips forward. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Exhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variation: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Deadlift or Good Mornings.

This will be your starting position.

Whilst keeping your knees stationary, inhale as you lean forwards from your waist (remember to keep a straight back), keeping the barbell close to you as you go. Keep lowering the barbell towards your feet until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.

After a brief pause bring your torso upright again by pushing your hips forward. Tip: Remember, this whole movement is performed with 'stiff' legs (slight bend at the knee, but no more) and a straight back throughout. Exhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Caution: This is not an exercise for those with back issues. Avoid jerking motions as this could cause serious injury.
Variation: Dumbbell Stiff Legged Deadlift or Good Mornings.
es: Coge una barra cargada y adopta una posición de pie con los pies separados a la altura de los hombros. Mantén los brazos frente a ti pero aún perpendiculares al suelo (agarre prono, con las palmas hacia atrás). Doble ligeramente las rodillas y mantenga la espalda recta en todo momento. Esta será tu posición inicial.

Mientras mantienes las rodillas quietas, inhala mientras te inclinas hacia adelante desde la cintura (recuerda mantener la espalda recta), manteniendo la barra cerca del cuerpo durante el movimiento. Sigue bajando la barra hacia tus pies hasta que sientas un estiramiento en los isquiosurales.

Después de una breve pausa, vuelva a enderezar el torso empujando las caderas hacia adelante. Consejo: Recuerde, todo este movimiento se realiza con las piernas "rígidas" (ligeramente doblada en la rodilla, pero no más) y la espalda recta en todo momento. Exhala mientras realizas esta parte del movimiento.
Repite hasta la cantidad recomendada de repeticiones.
Precaución: este no es un ejercicio para personas con problemas de espalda. Evite movimientos bruscos ya que esto podría causar lesiones graves.
Variación: Peso muerto con piernas rígidas con mancuernas o Buenos días.

name: Barbell Step Ups (Alternating)
instructions: Place a barbell across the back of your shoulders and adopt a shoulder width stance in front of a step. The height of the step should be realistic to the ability of the user (make sure you have both hands on the barbell for stability). Maintain a straight back throughout the whole movement so that you minimise risk of injury. This will be your starting position.

Place your right foot on the step/bench. Step up onto the step/bench by pushing through your right heel. As you do so, your hip and knee joints should straighten out. Lift your left leg onto the step/bench at this point. Exhale as you perform this part of the movement.
Step off the step/bench with your left leg by bending at the hip and knee joints of the right leg, inhale as you do so. Return to the starting position by taking your right foot off the step/bench.
Alternate as you go, therefore lead with you left leg on the second repetition.
Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions.
Variations: Dumbbell Step Ups or Barbell Step Ups.
